Xulgath gutragers exhibit more extreme developments along the same physiological path as their [bilebearer](/Monsters.aspx?ID=506) cousins. They are stockier and faster, with necks and limbs corded with muscle. They are also denser, with multiple stomachs full of fluids even more caustic than those of the bilebearer. Like the bilebearer, gutragers lack the distinctive stench typical of most xulgaths. A gutrager’s torso conceals its most disturbing feature—a densely coiled esophagus. Pressurized bladders and a muscular throat allow the gutrager to propel its esophagus outward in an explosion of yellow flesh and acidic bile; caustic stomach acid coating the esophagus makes even brief physical contact with the organ dangerous. As a last resort, the gutrager can intentionally accelerate the corrosion in its esophagus, releasing vile acid in a burst that also ends its life. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 27 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 [Xulgath Gutrager](/Monsters.aspx?ID=535) **Source** [Pathfinder #154: Siege of the Dinosaurs](/Sources.aspx?ID=31) pg. 85 **Perception** +19; darkvision **Languages** [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Undercommon](/Languages.aspx?ID=11) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +22, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +22, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +20 **Str** +4 **Dex** +6 **Con** +7 **Int** -2 **Wis** +1 **Cha** -1 --- **AC** 28 **Fort** +23 **Ref** +22 **Will** +17 **HP** 190 **Resistances** acid 10 **Caustic Fog** ([acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=3), [aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206)) 10 feet. Whenever a gutrager is within another xulgath’s stench aura, a fog appears around the gutrager; creatures in the fog (including the gutrager) can see as normal, but they are [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) from creatures outside the fog. Non-xulgaths that begin their turn in the fog take 2d6 acid damage. Strong winds suppress this ability.
**Self-Detonate** ([acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=3)) **Requirements** The gutrager has 45 or fewer HP; **Effect** The gutrager compresses its gut and explodes in a burst of viscera, dying instantly and dealing 8d8 acid damage to creatures and unattended objects in a 20-foot emanation centered on the gutrager (DC 31 basic Reflex save). --- **Speed** 40 feet **Melee** jaws +21, **Damage** 2d6+8 piercing plus 2d6 acid **Melee** claw +23 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)), **Damage** 2d8+8 slashing **Ranged** bile jet +23 ([Acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=3), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 4d8+7 acid **Corrosive Kiss** ([Acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=3)) **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The gutrager propels its esophagus out of its body to deliver an acidic blow. It makes an unarmed Strike against a creature or unattended object within 30 feet with a +23 attack bonus. On a hit, the target takes 2d6+8 bludgeoning plus 4d6 acid damage. On a critical hit, the target also takes 2d6 [persistent acid damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). The gutrager is [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3) for 1 round as it re-coils its esophagus.
Gutrager Origins Gutragers have been part of xulgath society for millennia. One xulgath legend suggests that xulgaths developed the material-strengthening art of inflection in order to safeguard clutch walls from gutragers’ powerful acid. Gutrager acid is also used to etch designs onto xulgath scales and, in difficult times, can be carefully used in place of fire to cook food.
name: Xulgath Gutrager

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Theletos](/Monsters.aspx?ID=536)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Theletoses maintain the balance between fate and free will. A theletos is a roughly spherical mass of crystals from which emerge four limbs, each split at the elbow and ending in three-fingered hands. A pair of crystalline tentacles also emerges from its body. Those who have been damaged by a theletos's tentacles describe a disorienting dilemma as they simultaneously feel forced to make a single choice while also being overwhelmed by the endless options available to choose from. Theletoses are more likely than most aeons to interfere in non-aeon societies, particularly in regions with draconian laws. Their involvement is twofold; a theletos concerns itself with both the freedom of individuals and the laws that restrict these individuals, even (or especially) when the two are in opposition.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Monitor](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 23

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Aeon_Theletos.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 7">[Theletos](/Monsters.aspx?ID=536)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="LN" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Aeon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=208" />
<trait label="Monitor" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=111" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 8

**Perception** +18; darkvision

**Languages**
envisioning

**Skills**
[Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +16, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +16, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +18, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +15

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +4

**Dex** +4

**Con** +3

**Int** +3

**Wis** +5

**Cha** +3
</row>

**Envisioning** When a theletos conveys information, it does so wordlessly through psychic projections. This acts as [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) with a range of 100 feet but is understandable to all creatures regardless of whether they have a language. The meaning to non-aeons can be vague and is often mysterious. A theletos can use this ability to communicate flawlessly with any other aeon on the same plane.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 25 

**Fort** +16 

**Ref** +13 

**Will** +18 

+1 status to all saves vs. magic
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 125

</row>

**Weaknesses**
chaotic 5

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet, fly 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +17 ([Lawful](/Traits.aspx?ID=99), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)),
**Damage** 2d10+4 bludgeoning plus 1d6 lawful

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
tentacle +17 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Lawful](/Traits.aspx?ID=99), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)),
**Damage** 2d8+4 slashing plus 1d6 lawful and fate drain

**Divine Innate Spells** DC 25, attack +17
- **4th**
[Augury](/Spells.aspx?ID=15) (at will), [Charm](/Spells.aspx?ID=34), [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=78), [Enthrall](/Spells.aspx?ID=104), [Outcast's Curse](/Spells.aspx?ID=211), [Remove Curse](/Spells.aspx?ID=250), [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=315), [Touch of Idiocy](/Spells.aspx?ID=341)

**Rituals** DC 25

- **3rd**
[Geas](/Rituals.aspx?ID=12)

**Fate Drain**  ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) A creature damaged by the theletos's tentacle must succeed at a DC 22 Will save or become [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37). As long as the creature is stupefied, it can no longer benefit from [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=76) effects. If the target fails additional saves against this ability, the condition value increases by 1 (to a maximum of stupefied 4). This condition value decreases by 1 every 24 hours.

**Wrath of Fate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=110)) The theletos releases a 60-foot cone of energy from its center. Creatures in the cone become overwhelmed with the knowledge of various fates that destiny has in store for them and lack of clear pathways to these potential futures. They must succeed at a DC 26 Will save or be [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) indefinitely. An affected creature can choose to roll twice when it attempts an attack, saving throw, or skill check and take the lower result. Regardless of the outcome, that creature is no longer slowed after that roll. The theletos can't use Wrath of Fate again for 1d4 rounds.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Maintaining the Balance</title>

Theletoses care little for the individuals and societies they manipulate, only that balance between freedom and fate is maintained. A theletos might help a creature who has lost their freedom escape, but it may also force those who swore to perform an unjust duty to stick to their word. If its plans are thwarted, a theletos doesn't seek revenge, but instead looks for other ways to redress the balance.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-2" />
name: Theletos

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Zelekhut](/Monsters.aspx?ID=537)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

A zelekhut is a bounty hunter and executioner. The zelekhut resembles a mechanical centaur crafted from silvery metal with golden clockwork wings adorning its back. Instead of hands, the zelekhut's arms end in long, barbed chains that crackle with electricity. Whether sent by the powers of Axis or summoned by mortal authorities, the zelekhut seeks out those who continually evade justice—either through active flight, or by abusing their power and station—so as to bring justice to the multiverse's most notorious fugitives and criminals. Indeed, many a zelekhut's quarry are as famous for their ability to evade capture as for the crimes they commit.

Though the zelekhut is implacable and unrelenting in enforcing sentences, it does not pass judgments of its own or take the initiative in pursuing targets of opportunity. While hunting a condemned serial killer or notorious thief across half a dozen planes, the zelekhut wouldn't shift a single hoof to capture a corrupt ruler whose offenses are far greater. All zelekhuts understand that laws can and must differ from place to place, and it is not the zelekhut's job to moralize, merely to obey its rightful assignments and track down those who seek to flee their punishment.

markdown:
<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Marut](/Monsters.aspx?ID=538)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

A marut is tasked with hunting mortals who cheat death by artificially extending their lifespans. This includes those who seek undeath, such as liches and vampires, but also includes those who use powerful magic to cling to their youth, use divination to discover and avoid an appointed death, or call too often on the power of resurrection. Once the marut has selected its target, the inevitable pursues its quarry without surcease or deviation until either it or the target is dead.

 Maruts seem to be carved from stone and clad in golden armor, yet they move with the deliberate grace of a creature made of flesh and bone. A marut never rushes, but its thunderous footfalls are relentless as it pursues its target. Though able to speak any language, the marut is taciturn even among inevitables.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Monitor](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 34

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Aeon_Marut.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 15">[Marut](/Monsters.aspx?ID=538)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="LN" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Large" />
<trait label="Aeon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=208" />
<trait label="Inevitable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=223" />
<trait label="Monitor" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=111" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 10

**Perception** +26; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [_true seeing_](/Spells.aspx?ID=344)

**Languages**
[Celestial](/Languages.aspx?ID=16), [Infernal](/Languages.aspx?ID=19), [Utopian](/Languages.aspx?ID=36); truespeech

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +31, [Axis Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +22, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +27, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +29, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +26, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +28

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +8

**Dex** +4

**Con** +6

**Int** +1

**Wis** +5

**Cha** +6
</row>

**Truespeech** A marut can speak with and understand any creature with a language.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 37 

**Fort** +27 

**Ref** +25 

**Will** +26 

+2 status to all saves vs. magic
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 230

, regeneration 15 (deactivated by chaotic)

</row>

**Immunities**
death effects, disease, emotion, poison, unconscious

**Weaknesses**
chaotic 15

[**Attack of Opportunity**](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3) <actions string="Reaction" />

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet; [_air walk_](/Spells.aspx?ID=6)

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +30 ([Lawful](/Traits.aspx?ID=99), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 3d8+11 bludgeoning plus 1d6 lawful and fists of thunder and lightning

**Divine Innate Spells** DC 37
- **4th**
[Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) (at will)
- **5th**
[Command](/Spells.aspx?ID=45), [Locate](/Spells.aspx?ID=173)
- **7th**
[Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=110), [Plane Shift](/Spells.aspx?ID=222), [Wall of Force](/Spells.aspx?ID=363)
- **8th**
[Chain Lightning](/Spells.aspx?ID=33), [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=78), [Earthquake](/Spells.aspx?ID=95), [Harm](/Spells.aspx?ID=146)
- **Constant (8th)**
[Air Walk](/Spells.aspx?ID=6), [True Seeing](/Spells.aspx?ID=344)

**Rituals** DC 36

- **3rd**
[Geas](/Rituals.aspx?ID=12)

**Final End**   The marut is anathema to beings that unnaturally extend their existence, including undead. Its fists bypass such creatures' resistances to damage and apply the creatures' highest weakness to damage. If a marut kills a creature that rejuvenates, like a lich or ghost, it always knows that the creature isn't fully defeated.

**Fists of Thunder and Lightning**  ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93)) Each time the marut makes a fist Strike, it chooses either lightning or thunder. If it chooses lightning, the attack deals an additional 2d12 electricity damage and the target must succeed at a DC 33 Fortitude save or be [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1) for 1 minute. If it chooses thunder, the attack deals an additional 3d8 sonic damage and the target must succeed at a DC 36 Fortitude save or be [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=8) for 1 minute.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_4_RelatedCreatures.png">Psychopomp Allies</title>

The mandate of marut inevitables is similar to that of [psychopomps](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=83). Generally, the practical psychopomps are content to let an unyielding marut complete its mission and swoop in afterward to ensure the work has been done, but occasionally, they may work together.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-2" />
name: Marut

The bythos is a guardian of space and time, and at all times seeks out those who misuse planar and temporal magic. A bythos is a roughly humanoid creature with four arms and a body made of swirling clouds and mist. Despite its appearance, its body feels like dry stone. A bythos seeks out paradoxes caused by irresponsible planar or dimensional travelers and repairs breaches where the barriers between planes have become thin or damaged. If the mortals responsible remain in the area and cannot be convinced to cease their activities, the bythos has no qualms about removing them. Using its ability to manipulate time, a bythos might cause an opponent to quickly die of old age as time speeds up around them, or cause a target to disappear from time and space. **[Recall Knowledge - Monitor](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 37 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32 [Bythos](/Monsters.aspx?ID=539) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 10 **Perception** +30; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** envisioning **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +32, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +29, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +25, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +25, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +30, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +29, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +30, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +26 **Str** +8 **Dex** +4 **Con** +5 **Int** +7 **Wis** +8 **Cha** +5 **Envisioning** When a bythos conveys information, it does so wordlessly through psychic projections. This acts as telepathy with a range of 100 feet but is understandable to all creatures regardless of whether they have a language. The meaning to non-aeons can be vague and is often mysterious. A bythos can use this ability to communicate flawlessly with any other aeon on the same plane. --- **AC** 39 **Fort** +25 **Ref** +26 **Will** +30 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 245 , regeneration 15 (deactivated by chaotic) **Weaknesses** chaotic 15 **Confusing Gaze** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)) 30 feet. A creature that ends its turn in the aura must attempt a DC 34 Will save. If it fails, it's [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5) for 1 round (or 1d4 rounds on a critical failure).
**Temporal Reversion** ([fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=76)) **Trigger** The bythos fails or critically fails a check; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The bythos rerolls the triggering check and takes the better result. --- **Speed** fly 35 feet **Melee** fist +32 ([Lawful](/Traits.aspx?ID=99), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d8+16 bludgeoning plus 2d8 cold and 2d8 lawful **Divine Innate Spells** DC 37 - **4th** [Dimensional Anchor](/Spells.aspx?ID=70) (at will) - **6th** [Slow](/Spells.aspx?ID=289) - **7th** [Dimensional Anchor](/Spells.aspx?ID=70), [Dimensional Lock](/Spells.aspx?ID=71), [Haste](/Spells.aspx?ID=147), [Plane Shift](/Spells.aspx?ID=222) - **8th** [Augury](/Spells.aspx?ID=15) (at will), [Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=338) **Rituals** DC 37 - **8th** [Imprisonment](/Rituals.aspx?ID=13) (temporal stasis only) **Aging Strikes** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) The bythos make two fist Strikes against a single target. If both Strikes hit, the target attempts a DC 37 Fortitude save. Creatures that don't get weaker with age or don't age are immune (GM's discretion). If a creature becomes [clumsy 4](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3), [drained 4](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10), and [enfeebled 4](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) due to Aging Strikes, it dies of old age.
**Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Failure** The creature becomes clumsy 1, drained 1, and enfeebled 1, or increases each of these conditions by 1. This effect is cumulative with other aging strikes from bythoses, to a maximum of clumsy 4, drained 4, and enfeebled 4.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the creature becomes clumsy 2, drained 2, and enfeebled 2, or increases these conditions by 2. **Focused Gaze** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) The bythos focuses its gaze on a creature it can see within 30 feet. The target must attempt a save against the bythos's confusing gaze. A bythos can't use this ability against the same creature more than once per turn. **Temporal Flurry** The bythos makes four fist Strikes. Its multiple attack penalty increases normally with each attack. **Temporal Strike** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=156)) The bythos touches a creature or object to displace it from time. The target attempts a DC 37 Fortitude save.
**Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** Time flows around the target; the target is [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) for 1 round.
**Failure** The target disappears from the present moment and reappears in the same location 1d4 rounds later as if no time had passed for it. If a creature or object occupies that space when the target returns, the target appears in the closest available space to its original location.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the target is slowed 1 for an extra 1d4 rounds after it returns.
Guardians of Time Bythos aeons have no innate ability to directly enter the mysterious [Dimension of Time](/Planes.aspx?ID=21), but many know of the hidden routes in the Great Beyond one can use to travel to this strange realm. A bythos prefers to destroy those who seek entrance to the Dimension of Time rather than risk the knowledge of how to reach it spreading too far.
name: Bythos

The ahuizotl is a vicious, semi-aquatic predator that resembles a hideous cross between a badger and an otter, with disturbingly web-fingered paws supplemented by a fifth hand at the end of a long, serpentine tail. A clever and stealthy hunter, the ahuizotl lures unwary prey to their doom by mimicking the cries of people in distress. The ahuizotl's macabre habit of feeding on a victim's eyes, fingernails, and teeth leaves the corpses of its kills uniquely mutilated. Some say the creatures consider these body parts delicacies, while others insist ahuizotls collect them as tribute to a powerful but unknown entity. The fact that an ahuizotl does not eat the actual flesh of its victims, instead depositing their savaged and waterlogged corpses in locations where the remains are sure to be found by friends or family, points to a third and perhaps more likely possibility—the ahuizotl simply enjoys using its violent dietary quirks to spread fear and despair. An ahuizotl walks on all fours, but its hands are capable of manipulating simple tools and other objects. Ahuizotls have roughly mustelid features and an extra membrane covering their eyes, giving their eyes a dull color suggestive of cataracts and somewhat blunting the creature's vision. Yet despite their bestial appearance, ahuizotls are nearly as intelligent as the average human, and wiser than most. Although they don't form societies of their own, they have been known to ally with violent cults or conclaves of monsters, and even to found small shrines and temples to sinister deities. The cult of Charon, the horseman of Death, is particularly popular among certain ahuizotls, who look forward to an afterlife spent wallowing in the waters of the River Styx. **[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 24 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 [Ahuizotl](/Monsters.aspx?ID=540) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 12 **Perception** +13; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +15, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +15, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +15 **Str** +5 **Dex** +3 **Con** +5 **Int** -1 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +3 **Voice Imitation** An ahuizotl can mimic the sounds of a person in distress by attempting a [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) check to [Lie](/Actions.aspx?ID=47). The ahuizotl has a +4 circumstance bonus to this check. --- **AC** 23 **Fort** +17 **Ref** +13 **Will** +13 **HP** 105 --- **Speed** 25 feet, swim 35 feet **Melee** jaws +17, **Damage** 2d8+8 piercing **Melee** claw +17 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 2d6+8 slashing **Melee** tail claw +17 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d4+8 slashing plus [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27) **Tail Drag** **Requirements** The ahuizotl has a Medium or smaller creature grabbed with its tail claw; **Effect** The ahuizotl attempts an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check against the creature's Fortitude DC.
**Critical Success** If the creature is 10 feet away from the ahuizotl, it is dragged into a square adjacent to the ahuizotl. The ahuizotl can make a jaws Strike against the creature.
**Success** If the creature is 10 feet away from the ahuizotl, it is dragged into a square adjacent to the ahuizotl.
**Failure** The creature is not dragged.
**Critical Failure** The creature is not dragged and the ahuizotl no longer has the creature grabbed.
Ahuizotl Allies An ahuizotl is unexpectedly canny in how it handles potential competitors in its territory, and when presented with fellow predators capable of conversation, it sometimes brokers alliances. [Will-o'- wisps](/Monsters.aspx?ID=414) are particular favorites as allies, as they can lure prey into the ahuizotl's clutches and feast on the anguish later when the mutilated corpse is discovered.
name: Ahuizotl

An akata is a hairless, blue-skinned quadruped with fearsome jaws, glowing eyes, a pair of thin tails, and a mane of writhing tentacles. It is a strangely silent beast, as it lacks the lungs and vocal chords necessary to vocalize. It also lacks ears, but it has heightened visual and olfactory senses. When food is scarce, an akata secretes a resin from its pores that forms into a sturdy cocoon of pale green crystal—the skymetal noqual. An akata can hibernate in this cocoon without needing to eat or drink for centuries, though it retains a rudimentary sense of its surroundings and can break out in only a few minutes' time. These cocoons allow the creatures to travel through the void of space, seeking new worlds where they can infect suitable humanoid hosts with their larval young. Once a victim succumbs to this infection, the offspring fight among themselves until one proves the strongest. The surviving akata then animates the corpse—now a [void zombie](/Monsters.aspx?ID=855)—which shambles about of its own accord. **[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 20 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15 [Akata](/Monsters.aspx?ID=541) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 13 **Perception** +6; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), no hearing, [scent (imprecise) 30 feet](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) **Languages** **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +7, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +7, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +7 **Str** +4 **Dex** +2 **Con** +4 **Int** -4 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +0 **Hibernation** After 3 or more days without eating, an akata can secrete resin that encases it in a noqual cocoon. The cocoon has Hardness 9, 40 HP, and a Broken Threshold of 18. It has resistance 5 to damage from magical sources. As long as the cocoon remains intact, the akata can't be harmed, and it doesn't need to eat or drink. While inside the cocoon, the akata gains [lifesense 30 feet](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=21). The akata remains in a state of hibernation until it is exposed to extreme heat or senses a living creature, at which point it can burst free of its cocoon in 1d4 minutes.
**No Hearing** An akata has no auditory senses. It is immune to [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16) effects, automatically critically fails Perception checks that require it to hear, and takes a –2 status penalty to Perception checks (but not initiative rolls) that involve sound but also rely on other senses. --- **AC** 16 **Fort** +9 **Ref** +5 **Will** +6 **HP** 15 **Immunities** disease **Resistances** fire 5, poison 5 **Weaknesses** salt water 5 **No Breath** An akata doesn't breathe and is immune to effects that require breathing (such as an [inhaled](/Traits.aspx?ID=96) poison).
**Salt Water Vulnerability** Salt water acts as an extremely strong acid to an akata. Full immersion in salt water deals 4d6 acid damage per round. --- **Speed** 30 feet, climb 15 feet **Melee** jaws +9 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 1d6+4 piercing plus void death **Void Death** ([Disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46)) An akata implants its parasitic larval young into any creature it bites, but only Medium or Small humanoids make suitable hosts; all other creatures are immune to this disease; **Saving Throw** DC 17 Fortitude; **Stage 1** carrier with no ill effect 1 (1 day); **Stage 2** [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) (1 day); **Stage 3** as stage 2 (1 day); **Stage 4** drained 2 and [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15) (1 day); **Stage 5** as stage 4 (1 day); **Stage 6** dead and corpse rises as a void zombie in 2d4 hours
Noqual Cocoons An akata's noqual cocoon, despite its crystalline appearance, is in fact made of a rare skymetal with a glassy green sheen. Noqual is as easy to work as iron but much lighter, and it is valued for its magic-resistant qualities, but the noqual from an akata's cocoon is very low quality and can't be used as a crafting material.
name: Akata

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Movanic Deva (Guardian Angel)](/Monsters.aspx?ID=542)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Movanic devas are stewards of the flow of good creatures' souls through the cycle of life and death, from the point a creature is born to the day it dies. These angels arise from the souls of stalwart nurturers and protectors, and share a specific connection with a creature's vital essence—its life force. Movanic devas are most concerned with guiding and protecting good creatures during their mortal lives, so once a soul has passed into the River of Souls, the movanic deva leaves its protection to the soul angels, or monadic devas. As part of their stewardship over good creatures, movanic devas can waken within animals a level of sapience to allow them to become kindly and benevolent as well, thus increasing the flow of goodness into the afterlife. They also spend time patrolling the [Positive Energy Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=3) and [Negative Energy Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=2), seeking to contain significant threats to life.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Celestial](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 27

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Angel_MovanicDeva.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 10">[Movanic Deva](/Monsters.aspx?ID=542)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="NG" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Angel" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=8" />
<trait label="Celestial" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=23" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 14

**Perception** +22; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12)

**Languages**
[Celestial](/Languages.aspx?ID=16), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Infernal](/Languages.aspx?ID=19); [_tongues_](/Spells.aspx?ID=340)

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +20, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +22, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +22, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +22, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +19, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +17, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +17

<row gap="medium">

**Dex** +4

**Con** +4

**Int** +4

**Wis** +5

**Cha** +5
</row>

**Items**
_+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [bastard sword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=15)_

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">

**Fort** +21 

**Ref** +17 

**Will** +19 

+1 to all saves vs. magic
</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

**Immunities**
negative

**Weaknesses**
evil 10

**Aura of Vitality** ([abjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=2), [aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48)) 20 feet. Allies in the movanic deva's aura gain a +1 status bonus to all saving throws, resistance 10 to positive and negative damage, and are unharmed by the effects of a plane's [positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128) and [negative](/Traits.aspx?ID=118) traits. Animals in the aura of 12th level or lower don't attack the movanic deva or the deva's allies unless they are controlled or otherwise forced to attack.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 30 feet, fly 40 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
flaming bastard sword +23 ([Good](/Traits.aspx?ID=83), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [two-hand d12](/Traits.aspx?ID=198)),
**Damage** 2d8+9 slashing plus 1d6 fire and 1d6 good

**Divine Innate Spells** DC 29
- **1st**
[Detect Alignment](/Spells.aspx?ID=65) (at will; evil only)
- **2nd**
[Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=164) (at will; self only)
- **4th**
[Create Food](/Spells.aspx?ID=52), [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=148) (×3)
- **5th**
[Divine Wrath](/Spells.aspx?ID=86), [Remove Curse](/Spells.aspx?ID=250), [Remove Disease](/Spells.aspx?ID=251), [Remove Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=252)

[Tongues](/Spells.aspx?ID=340)

- **1st**
[Angelic Messenger](/Rituals.aspx?ID=22)

[Awaken Animal](/Rituals.aspx?ID=3)

**Dispelling Field** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The movanic deva attempts to unravel unwelcome magic effects on allies within its aura of vitality to protect them from malevolent forces. The movanic deva attempts a counteract check against as many spell effects affecting allies in the area as it wishes with a +19 counteract modifier and a counteract level of 4, rolling once for the selected effects.

**Flaming Armaments**  ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) When a movanic deva wields a weapon, that weapon gains the effect of a [flaming](/Equipment.aspx?ID=295) rune.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">The Gift of Sapience</title>

When a movanic deva awakens an [animal](/Traits.aspx?ID=9), the animal becomes neutral good and is well-disposed toward the deva, but the deva understands that free will is an important part of life. While the deva provides enough support and guidance to the animal to help it adjust to its newly sapient world view, not all animals awakened in this way remain on the path of good.
</aside>

Monadic devas stand vigil along the River of Souls as it passes from the mortal realm into the [Ethereal Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=9). Those who seek to fish souls out for their own use stalk this metaphysical river, and it is against these predators that monadic devas fight. At other times, monadic devas work to guide newcomers to the River of Souls who might be in danger of drifting from the current and lingering as ghosts. Monadic devas often find their tasks place them in alliances with [psychopomps](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=83), and they work well with the monitors despite the philosophical divide between their views on a soul's inherent goodness. A monadic deva is 7 feet tall and weighs 220 pounds. **[Recall Knowledge - Celestial](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 30 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 [Monadic Deva](/Monsters.aspx?ID=543) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 15 **Perception** +25; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Celestial](/Languages.aspx?ID=16), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Infernal](/Languages.aspx?ID=19); [_tongues_](/Spells.aspx?ID=340) **Skills** [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +25, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +24, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +22, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +20, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +25, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +22 **Str** +7 **Dex** +4 **Con** +5 **Int** +4 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +5 **Items** _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [mace](/Weapons.aspx?ID=7)_ --- **AC** 33 **Fort** +24 **Ref** +21 **Will** +20 +1 to all saves vs. magic **HP** 245 **Immunities** death effects **Weaknesses** evil 10 **Spiritual Warden** ([abjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=2), [aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48)) 20 feet. Allies in the aura gain a +2 status bonus to saving throws against [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects and effects that target or manipulate their souls. --- **Speed** 30 feet, fly 60 feet **Melee** holy mace +28 ([Good](/Traits.aspx?ID=83), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Shove](/Traits.aspx?ID=193)), **Damage** 2d6+15 bludgeoning plus 1d6 force and 1d6 good plus solid blow **Divine Innate Spells** DC 31 - **1st** [Detect Alignment](/Spells.aspx?ID=65) (at will; evil only) - **2nd** [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=164) (at will; self only) - **4th** [Charm](/Spells.aspx?ID=34) - **5th** [Creation](/Spells.aspx?ID=54), [Remove Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=252) - **6th** [Divine Wrath](/Spells.aspx?ID=86), [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=148), [Paralyze](/Spells.aspx?ID=213), [Remove Curse](/Spells.aspx?ID=250), [Remove Disease](/Spells.aspx?ID=251) - **Constant (5th)** [Tongues](/Spells.aspx?ID=340) **Rituals** DC 32 - **1st** [Angelic Messenger](/Rituals.aspx?ID=22) **Holy Armaments** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65)) Any weapon gains the effect of a [holy](/Equipment.aspx?ID=299) property rune while a monadic deva wields it. **Rebuke Soul** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Good](/Traits.aspx?ID=83), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93)) The monadic deva speaks a word to cause a creature's soul to recoil at its sins or an undead creature to recoil at its lack of a soul. One non-good living or undead target within 40 feet takes 5d10 good damage and must attempt a DC 32 Fortitude save. Regardless of the outcome, the target is then temporarily immune for 10 minutes.
**Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target takes half damage and is [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36).
**Failure** The target takes full damage and is stunned 2.
**Critical Failure** The target takes double damage and is stunned 4. **Solid Blow** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75)) When a monadic deva hits a target for the second time during its turn with its mace, it deals an additional 2d12 force damage as its weapon shimmers with ripples of power.
name: Monadic Deva

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Planetar (Justice Angel)](/Monsters.aspx?ID=544)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The beings of righteous fury known as planetars are known to be the least patient of angels. They exist to destroy evil that cannot be redeemed, and they often leave prolonged dialogue to other angels. Of course, no planetar ignores the importance of diplomacy, but they know that others among the angelic hosts are better suited to that task, while they focus on the larger picture. In angelic armies, planetars serve as commanders or generals. They stand at least 9 feet tall and weigh over 500 pounds.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Celestial](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 37

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Angel_Planetar.png" />
</column>

</row>

---

**Ref** +25 

**Will** +28 

+1 status to all saves vs. magic
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 300

, regeneration 15 (deactivated by evil)

</row>

**Weaknesses**
evil 15

**Aura of Righteousness** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65)) 20 feet. Allies in the planetar's aura gain a +2 status bonus to AC against evil creatures and a +2 status bonus to damage rolls against evil creatures. The area in the aura is difficult terrain for evil creatures.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet, fly 60 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_holy greatsword_ +32 ([Good](/Traits.aspx?ID=83), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)),
**Damage** 3d12+16 slashing plus 1d6 good

**Divine Innate Spells** DC 37, attack +29
- **2nd**
[Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=164) (at will; self only), [Remove Paralysis](/Spells.aspx?ID=253)
- **5th**
[Breath of Life](/Spells.aspx?ID=29), [Death Ward](/Spells.aspx?ID=64), [Freedom of Movement](/Spells.aspx?ID=128), [Restoration](/Spells.aspx?ID=258) (×3)
- **6th**
[Blade Barrier](/Spells.aspx?ID=24), [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=78) (at will)
- **7th**
[Charm](/Spells.aspx?ID=34), [Plane Shift](/Spells.aspx?ID=222), [Power Word Blind](/Spells.aspx?ID=226), [Remove Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=252) (at will)
- **8th**
[Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=78), [Divine Wrath](/Spells.aspx?ID=86), [Earthquake](/Spells.aspx?ID=95), [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=148), [Power Word Stun](/Spells.aspx?ID=228), [Sunburst](/Spells.aspx?ID=326)
- **Constant (5th)**
[Detect Alignment](/Spells.aspx?ID=65) (evil only), [Tongues](/Spells.aspx?ID=340), [True Seeing](/Spells.aspx?ID=344)

**Rituals** DC 37

- **1st**
[Angelic Messenger](/Rituals.aspx?ID=22)
- **5th**
[Call Spirit](/Rituals.aspx?ID=5), [Resurrect](/Rituals.aspx?ID=20)

**Blade of Justice** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The planetar makes a greatsword Strike against a target it detects as evil. If the target is evil, the Strike deals three extra weapon damage dice and deals 1d6 [persistent](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) good damage to the target. The planetar can convert all the physical damage from the attack into good damage.

**[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=8)** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) The planetar can take on the appearance of any Small or Medium humanoid. This doesn't change their Speed or Strikes.

**Holy Armaments**  ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65)) Any weapon gains the effect of a [holy](/Equipment.aspx?ID=299) property rune while a planetar wields it.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Planetar Justice</title>

Planetars know pathways to travel between many planes of existence, and they use these frequently to deliver messages, warnings, and ultimatums to those whose actions have attracted angelic concern. If a situation can be resolved with swift on-the-spot justice, a planetar takes that route in hopes of staving off an escalation of violence.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-3" />
name: Planetar

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Solar (Archangel)](/Monsters.aspx?ID=545)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Solars are among the greatest of all angels, ranking just below empyreal lords. Many solars serve at the right hand of a deity or champion a cause that benefits an entire world. Most archangels resemble humanoids, but not all—they can adopt more unusual forms as needed. A typical solar is about 9 feet tall and weighs about 500 pounds, with a commanding voice that is impossible to ignore. Most have silvery or golden skin.

Solars are legendary trackers; the most masterful of them are said to be able to follow the centuries-old passage of a pit fiend flying through the Astral Plane.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Celestial](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 51

The bonus increases to +4 against control by evil creatures and attacks by evil summoned creatures. When the solar or an ally is hit by an attack from a creature in the aura, that foe must succeed at a DC 43 Will save or be blinded for 1 minute (this is an [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93) effect). It's then temporarily immune for 1 minute.

**Animate Weapon** <actions string="Reaction" />  **Trigger** The solar hits with a melee weapon Strike while Animate Weapon is not already in effect; **Effect** The solar's weapon leaps into the air and moves with the solar as if held with both hands. The weapon gains the effects of the [dancing](/Equipment.aspx?ID=293) weapon rune for 1 minute.

**Arrow of Mortality**   The solar doesn't need to provide ammunition for their bow. When they draw their bow, they create a magical arrow of light that sheds bright light in a 20-foot radius until the end of the solar's next turn. On a critical hit with the arrow, if the target has 75 or fewer Hit Points after taking the damage, it drops to 0 Hit Points and becomes [dying 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=11) (or, if it was already dying, increases its dying value by 3).

**[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=8)** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) The solar can take on the appearance of any Small or Medium humanoid. This doesn't change their Speed or Strikes.

**Holy Armaments**  ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65)) Any weapon gains the effect of a [holy](/Equipment.aspx?ID=299) property rune while a solar wields it.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Solar Genesis</title>

Unlike most angels, solars are created from divine energy, sometimes blended with good souls. They are joined in this distinction by the ancient empyrean angels.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-3" />

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Animate Dream](/Monsters.aspx?ID=546)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

An animate dream coalesces from centuries of stray fragments of reverie and dream left behind as slumbering minds drift through the [Dimension of Dreams](/Traits.aspx?ID=299). In most cases, these fragments simply disperse on their own, but when dreamers flee from nightmares back to the waking world, these fragments can take on a supernatural life of their own. They have little reasoning and intent except to seek similar fragments, absorb them, and grow. When enough of them gather together, they merge into a horrific form as an animate dream.

Individually, animate dreams can hold a wide range of appearances, but most take on rudimentary shapes akin to the living forms of the minds that spawned them. Animate dreams that rise from slumbering humanoids, the most commonly encountered, thus appear humanoid, but with distorted, nightmarish shapes that incorporate additional twisted limbs and frightening features. In these forms, animate dreams find their way out of the Dreamlands and into the waking world, only to discover they have no way of returning and suffer a relentless hunger that only new nightmares can sate.

An animate dream thrives on the terror fueled by nightmares and fear and uses its supernatural abilities to invoke such feelings in the minds of its victims, cursing its prey by creating a permanent link to that individual's dreams. Thereafter, the victim is subjected to all manner of reoccurring nightmares. As the nightmares continue, the victim finds it more and more difficult to remain awake. In the end, the victim plunges into an accursed slumber from which it cannot return. These victims provide the animate dream with the purest form of fear and are its preferred sustenance.

An animate dream can subsist on the ambient fear generated by regular nightmares, but these lesser fears aren't as satisfying, often driving the animate dream to seek out new victims. Left without a source of fear, an animate dream won't starve to death, but it will grow increasingly violent and desperate. Famished animate dreams are the ones most often encountered, as they have been forced to abandon subtlety to pursue more blatant methods of harvesting fear. Yet such animate dreams also tend to be the least powerful of their kind. Those who have grown metaphysically fat on fear have generally done so by adopting a specific nightmare theme, such as being buried alive, being swept away by ocean waves, or being consumed by wild beasts. Such animate dreams often have similarly themed innate spells to augment their powers.

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Ankou](/Monsters.aspx?ID=547)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Ankous are shadowy killers who serve powerful fey creatures, or even the Eldest— the demigods of the First World. Amid the courts of the Eldest or of powerful fey rulers, these sinister assassins lurk overhead as they await the call to action—implied threats akin to deadly weapons hung as decor in a royal hall. Ankous never speak aloud; when they feel the need to communicate at all, they do so in a telepathic whisper directly into a victim's mind. While an ankou's body is made of strange, fleshy material, their claws and the cruel hooks on their wings are made of razor-sharp cold iron, a quality that makes them much feared among other fey. A typical ankou is 10 feet tall and has an 8-foot wingspan but weighs less than 80 pounds.

While most ankous serve powerful masters, some of these murderous fey are left to their own devices and serve none but their own capricious, cruel whims. In some cases, their master may have been slain, while in others the ankou may have been released from service for any number of reasons. An ankou left to indulge their own whims unrestrained is often the most dangerous ankou of them all!

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27

Shadow doubles have the same statistics as an ankou, but they have the [summoned](/Traits.aspx?ID=154) trait, have 84 Hit Points, can't use Shadow Doubles or innate spells, and have an attack bonus of +25 for their Strikes. A shadow double that attempts a saving throw against a [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=100) effect can't get a result better than failure. Each double remains for 1 round, until it's reduced to 0 Hit Points, or until it moves further than 120 feet from the ankou, whichever comes first. Each round thereafter, the ankou can spend a single action that has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait to extend the duration of surviving duplicates by 1 round, to a maximum duration of 1 minute. The ankou can see through the eyes of all of the shadow doubles at once. A character who [Seeks](/Actions.aspx?ID=84) can identify an ankou as real or a shadow double with a successful DC 39 Perception check.

</aside>

<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_3_Locations.png">Ant Hives</title>

Giant ants form vast underground colonies, excavating deep burrows or infesting existing caverns. Ants are omnivorous and cultivate fungus farms, but they are happy to eat whatever presents itself. Humanoids and their domesticated animals are easy fuel for the insectile machinery of their hives. Worker ants lack the sting of their warrior cousins, while elite drones fly on gossamer wings (fly Speed of 30 feet) to seek new food sources for their queen.
</aside>

markdown:
Aolazes are great beasts carved from stone and metal and magically imbued with the essence of life. The exact means of their creation is a long-lost secret, and they are so rare that scholars have little opportunity to study active specimens. The best-known aolazes are museum pieces or battlefield relics destroyed or deactivated centuries ago, though fragmented records suggest that many more were made and might remain, yet to be unearthed. Most aolazes are built in the shape of great land-bound beasts, such as elephants, rhinoceroses, or dinosaurs. Regardless of the specific creature an aolaz has been constructed to resemble, it is not bound to walk the earth like its inspirations are—it's imbued with the magical ability to pursue across water and even through the air. Few can escape an aolaz's wrath once it is earned. **[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4)): DC 43 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 41 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 38 [Aolaz](/Monsters.aspx?ID=550) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 21 **Perception** +33; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=23), flawless hearing **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +35 **Str** +9 **Dex** +4 **Con** +8 **Int** -4 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +3 **Flawless Hearing** An aolaz has an incredible sense of hearing. It can hear any sound made within 1,000 feet as though it were only 5 feet away from the source of the sound, and any sound within 1 mile as though it were only 30 feet away from the source of the sound. An aolaz's hearing is a [precise sense](/Rules.aspx?ID=411). --- **AC** 42 **Fort** +35 **Ref** +27 **Will** +31 **HP** 255 **Immunities** bleed, [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188) attacks, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34), sonic, [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Resistances** physical 15 (except [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=271)) --- **Speed** 50 feet, [_air walk_](/Spells.aspx?ID=6), [_water walk_](/Spells.aspx?ID=371) **Melee** trunk +35 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192), [Sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=194), [Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=196)), **Damage** 5d10+17 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Melee** foot +33 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 5d8+15 bludgeoning **Arcane Innate Spells** DC 40 - **Constant (9th)** [Air Walk](/Spells.aspx?ID=6), [Water Walk](/Spells.aspx?ID=371) **Roll** The aolaz tucks its head down and rolls up into an armored sphere. While Rolling, an aolaz has AC 44, Fort +37, Ref +29, Will +33, and Speed 100 feet, but it can't use its trunk Strikes or its Ultrasonic Blast. It can make foot Strikes while rolling, but only as part of a [Trample](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=39). The aolaz can use this action again to unroll and resume its standing form. **[Trample](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=39)** Huge or smaller, foot, DC 40 **Ultrasonic Blast** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=147)) The aolaz releases a tremendous blast of sonic energy from its trunk in a 150-foot line, dealing 12d10 sonic damage. The frequency of this sound is such that it is completely imperceptible to [humanoids](/Traits.aspx?ID=91), but the damage it wreaks is all too evident. Each creature in the area must attempt a DC 40 Fortitude save. The aolaz can't use Ultrasonic Blast again for 1d4 rounds.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes half damage and is [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36).
**Failure** The creature takes full damage and is stunned 2.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes double damage and is stunned 3.
Jistkan Behemoths Thousands of years ago, the Jistka Imperium mastered the art of construct creation, and the aolaz represents the height of its craft. The Jistkans used primal magic to imbue their constructs with spirits of nature. However, when Jistkan creators turned to the outer planes, and to fiends in particular, as a source to power even greater constructs, they unknowingly orchestrated their own doom.
name: Aolaz

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Hound Archon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=551)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Among the rank-and-file soldiers of Heaven's armies, few individuals are more dependable and truer to the cause than the noble hound archons, embodiments of the virtue of diligence. Unlike [horned archons](/Monsters.aspx?ID=31), who perform at a similar level along Heaven's grand hierarchy, hound archons do not scout ahead for evil or perform reconnaissance missions in hostile territory. A hound archon's role is as a soldier in the heart of battle or, occasionally, as a sentinel tasked with protecting a particular strategic location or holy site.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Celestial](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 19

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Archon_HoundArchon.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 4">[Hound Archon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=551)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="LG" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Archon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=13" />
<trait label="Celestial" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=23" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 22

**Perception** +13; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12)

**Languages**
[Celestial](/Languages.aspx?ID=16), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Infernal](/Languages.aspx?ID=19); [_tongues_](/Spells.aspx?ID=340)

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +12, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +10, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +9, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +10

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +4

**Dex** +2

**Con** +4

**Int** +0

**Wis** +3

**Cha** +2
</row>

**Items**
[Greatsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=24), [Full Plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=13)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 22 

**Fort** +14 

**Ref** +8 

**Will** +11 

+1 status to all saves vs. magic
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 70

</row>

**Weaknesses**
evil 5

**[Retributive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=31)** <actions string="Reaction" /> 

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 35 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
greatsword +14 ([Good](/Traits.aspx?ID=83), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)),
**Damage** 1d12+6 slashing plus 1d6 good

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws +14 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Good](/Traits.aspx?ID=83), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)),
**Damage** 1d6+6 piercing plus 1d6 good

**Divine Innate Spells** DC 19
- **Cantrips (2nd)**
[Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=190)
- **4th**
[Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69)
- **Constant (5th)**
[Tongues](/Spells.aspx?ID=340)

**Archon's Door**   Once per day, if an archon sees another creature cast [_dimension door_](/Spells.aspx?ID=69), the archon can use dimension door (heightened to 5th level) within 1 round to attempt to follow that creature to the maximum distance of the archon's dimension door. If the archon's dimension door has enough distance, the archon appears the same distance and direction from the creature as before either creature used dimension door.

**[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=8)** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) A hound archon can take the appearance of any canid [animal](/Traits.aspx?ID=9) of Small to Large size. This doesn't change their Speed or the attack and damage bonuses for their Strikes, but if the canid's bite attack has the [Knockdown](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=20) ability, then the hound archon's bite attack gains that ability while they are in that form.

**Diligent Assault** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The hound archon carefully makes one greatsword Strike and one jaws Strike in any order. These attacks count toward the hound archon's multiple attack penalty, but the multiple attack penalty doesn't increase until after all the attacks.

</column>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-9" />
name: Hound Archon

Trumpet archons are messengers, emissaries, and storytellers—embodiments of the virtue of kindness. They use stories and allegories to prevent bloodshed, and among all archons they are the most likely to work together with angels, perhaps leading to the similarities in their forms. When diplomacy is impossible, trumpet archons rally the righteous to battle—one reassuring glance from a trumpet archon is often all it takes to instill a shaken ally with resounding courage once again. **[Recall Knowledge - Celestial](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 32 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27 [Trumpet Archon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=552) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 23 **Perception** +26; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Celestial](/Languages.aspx?ID=16), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Infernal](/Languages.aspx?ID=19); [_tongues_](/Spells.aspx?ID=340) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +25, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +29, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +28, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +23, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +23, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) +31, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +25 **Str** +7 **Dex** +5 **Con** +5 **Int** +3 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +8 **Items** _+2 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [bastard sword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=15)_, _+1 [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=279) [full plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=13)_, [virtuoso trumpet](/Equipment.aspx?ID=37) --- **AC** 36 **Fort** +24 **Ref** +24 **Will** +27 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 285 **Resistances** sonic 15 **Weaknesses** evil 15 **[Retributive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=31)** --- **Speed** 35 feet, fly 60 feet **Melee** bastard sword +29 ([Good](/Traits.aspx?ID=83), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 2d12+13 slashing plus 2d6 sonic and 1d6 good **Divine Innate Spells** DC 34 - **Cantrips (6th)** [Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=190) - **3rd** [Circle of Protection](/Spells.aspx?ID=38) (against evil only; ×2) - **4th** [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) (at will) - **5th** [Banishment](/Spells.aspx?ID=19), [Breath of Life](/Spells.aspx?ID=29) - **6th** [Heroism](/Spells.aspx?ID=149), [Zealous Conviction](/Spells.aspx?ID=378) - **7th** [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=148) (×2), [Sound Burst](/Spells.aspx?ID=292) - **Constant (5th)** [Tongues](/Spells.aspx?ID=340) **Archon's Door** Once per day, if an archon sees another creature cast [_dimension door_](/Spells.aspx?ID=69), the archon can use dimension door (heightened to 5th level) within 1 round to attempt to follow that creature to the maximum distance of the archon's dimension door. If the archon's dimension door has enough distance, the archon appears the same distance and direction from the creature as before either creature used dimension door. **Kind Word** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=101), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) The trumpet archon offers an ally praise or encouragement. The archon attempts to counteract one [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60) effect on the ally (with a counteract modifier of +24), and the archon and the ally both gain a +1 status bonus to attack rolls and saving throws for 1 round. **Trumpet Blast** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) The trumpet archon blows mightily on their horn, creating a tone of such beauty and grandeur those who hear it are paralyzed in awe. Non-archons within 100 feet must attempt a DC 34 Fortitude save. They are temporarily immune for 10 minutes.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16).
**Failure** The creature is [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36) and flat-footed as long as it's stunned.
**Critical Failure** The creature is [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28) for 1 round.
Instruments of War A trumpet archon's magical instrument is no mere piece of brass; each of these immaculately crafted horns is said to be crafted in Heaven's blessed forges and infused with the voices of a thousand angels. Only archons can truly play such trumpets—mortals lack the power necessary to create trumpet archons' iconic clarion calls, but can still play them as virtuoso instruments.
name: Trumpet Archon

markdown:
Embodiments of the virtue of prudence, star archons serve as philosophers and administrators, and in times of war as the tacticians, strategists, and generals of archon armies. Star archons burn with the glory and intensity of a sun, and their sense of duty and desire to defeat evil are as indefatigable as the light of the stars. While their dazzling intellects and unmatched strategic cunning make them most useful in war rooms rather than on battlefields, star archons are fierce opponents when motivated to enter the fray. Those who witness the self-sacrifice of a star archon and live to tell the tale describe the experience in terms that might befit the sight of a supernova at the instant the star collapses in on itself. **[Recall Knowledge - Celestial](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 39 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 37 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 34 [Star Archon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=553) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 23 **Perception** +35; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [_true seeing_](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) **Languages** [Celestial](/Languages.aspx?ID=16), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Infernal](/Languages.aspx?ID=19); [_tongues_](/Spells.aspx?ID=340) **Skills** [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +33, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +37, [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +31, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +33, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +33, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +33, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +37, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +33, [Warfare Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +39 **Str** +8 **Dex** +9 **Con** +6 **Int** +9 **Wis** +9 **Cha** +6 **Items** _+2 [greater resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=279) [full plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=13)_, _+2 [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [returning](/Equipment.aspx?ID=301) [starknife](/Weapons.aspx?ID=44)_ --- **AC** 43 **Fort** +31 **Ref** +34 **Will** +34 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 400 **Resistances** fire 15 **Weaknesses** evil 15 **Blinding Soul** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=100), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)) Whenever the star archon takes slashing damage, bright light pours from their wounds. Non-archons within 10 feet must attempt a DC 38 Fortitude save. On a failure, a creature is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) for 1 round. On a critical failure, the creature is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1) for 1d4 rounds instead. The creature is then temporarily immune for 1 round.
**Explosive Rebirth** ([death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), [good](/Traits.aspx?ID=83)) When killed, the star archon explodes in a blinding flash of holy energy that deals 12d6 fire damage and 12d6 good damage to anything in a 100-foot emanation, with a DC 40 basic Reflex save. A non-archon creature that sees the explosion and critically fails its save is permanently [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1) as well. The slain star archon reincarnates 1d4 rounds later as a [shield archon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=33).
**[Retributive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=31)** A star archon can also make a Retributive Strike by throwing its starknife, and the enemy and ally can be within 60 feet instead of 15 feet. --- **Speed** 35 feet, fly 75 feet **Melee** returning starknife +38 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [deadly 1d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Good](/Traits.aspx?ID=83), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 3d4+16 piercing plus 2d6 good and 3d6 fire **Ranged** starknife +38 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [deadly 1d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Good](/Traits.aspx?ID=83), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [thrown 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=195), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 3d4+16 piercing plus 2d6 good and 3d6 fire **Divine Innate Spells** DC 43 - **4th** [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) (at will) - **5th** [Sending](/Spells.aspx?ID=272) - **7th** [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=148), [Prismatic Spray](/Spells.aspx?ID=233) - **9th** [Blindness](/Spells.aspx?ID=26), [Implosion](/Spells.aspx?ID=162), [Sunburst](/Spells.aspx?ID=326) - **Constant (5th)** [Tongues](/Spells.aspx?ID=340) - **Constant (6th)** [True Seeing](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) **Archon's Door** Once per day, if an archon sees another creature cast dimension door, the archon can use [_dimension door_](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) (heightened to 5th level) within 1 round to attempt to follow that creature to the maximum distance of the archon's dimension door. If the archon's dimension door has enough distance, the archon appears the same distance and direction from the creature as before either creature used dimension door. **Prudent Asterism** The star archon calculates a celestial constellation and sends their starknife flying along that path from one foe to the next. It makes a starknife Strike against a target within 60 feet. If the Strike hits, it can make another Strike at a different target within 60 feet of the first target, and so on, until it misses with a Strike or runs out of targets it hasn't attacked within range of the most recent target. The star archon can attack a given target only once per use of this ability. These attacks don't apply any range increment penalty, and the star archon resolves all the attacks before increasing the multiple attack penalty.
name: Star Archon

markdown:
The mightiest of archon-kind form in the heat of battle from incredibly uncommon circumstances. When a gathering of [lantern archons](/Monsters.aspx?ID=30) converges into a gestalt in order to defend allies in a fight that seems all but lost, a fellow archon of significant strength—such as a [star archon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=553)—can commit a brave act of self-sacrifice by dimensionally transposing itself with the gestalt. If the circumstances are just right and luck is with the archons, the result is not a glorious sacrifice, but instead the earth-rocking emergence of a bastion archon. In this way, the lowliest of archons—the lanterns—can, in an instant, become their kind's greatest hope. Bastion archons are massive beings made of the very stone of Heaven's Holy Mountain, with their visages obscured by a blinding light surrounding the gestalt that originally formed the bastion. Tireless in their embodiment of the virtue of sacrifice, a bastion archon almost always protects the area where they manifested, and even when they are destroyed they leave an indelible mark of righteousness upon the surrounding region. **[Recall Knowledge - Celestial](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 45 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 43 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 40 [Bastion Archon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=554) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 24 **Perception** +37; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [_true seeing_](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) **Languages** [Celestial](/Languages.aspx?ID=16), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Infernal](/Languages.aspx?ID=19); [_tongues_](/Spells.aspx?ID=340) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +38, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +34, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +34, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +32 **Str** +10 **Dex** +7 **Con** +10 **Int** +6 **Wis** +8 **Cha** +6 --- **AC** 47 **Fort** +38 **Ref** +31 **Will** +34 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 280 ([fast healing 30](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=15)) **Weaknesses** evil 15 **Bastion Aura** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [good](/Traits.aspx?ID=83), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) 50 feet. All good-aligned creatures in the aura have [fast healing 30](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=15) for as long as they remain in range, and they gain a +2 status bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls.
**Ultimate Sacrifice** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [good](/Traits.aspx?ID=83), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) If the bastion archon is slain by an evil creature, it explodes in a geyser of holy light, restoring 140 HP to all good-aligned creatures in a 40-foot emanation. The ground in the affected area is also subjected to a level 10 [consecrate](/Rituals.aspx?ID=8) ritual, and the ground is consecrated for 10 years (or only 24 hours if the ultimate sacrifice took place in [Hell](/Planes.aspx?ID=18), [Abaddon](/Planes.aspx?ID=12), or the [Abyss](/Planes.aspx?ID=13)).
**[Retributive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=31)** --- **Speed** 70 feet; [_air walk_](/Spells.aspx?ID=6) **Melee** fist +40 ([Good](/Traits.aspx?ID=83), [Lawful](/Traits.aspx?ID=99), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 4d8+20 bludgeoning plus 2d6 good **Divine Innate Spells** DC 42, attack +34 - **4th** [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) (at will) - **7th** [Prismatic Spray](/Spells.aspx?ID=233), [True Target](/Spells.aspx?ID=346) (x3), [Vibrant Pattern](/Spells.aspx?ID=357) - **10th** [Meteor Swarm](/Spells.aspx?ID=191), [Polar Ray](/Spells.aspx?ID=224), [Sunburst](/Spells.aspx?ID=326) - **Constant (10th)** [Air Walk](/Spells.aspx?ID=6), [True Seeing](/Spells.aspx?ID=344), [Tongues](/Spells.aspx?ID=340) **Archon's Door** Once per day, if an archon sees another creature cast [_dimension door_](/Spells.aspx?ID=69), the archon can use dimension door (heightened to 5th level) within 1 round to attempt to follow that creature to the maximum distance of the archon's dimension door. If the archon's dimension door has enough distance, the archon appears the same distance and direction from the creature as before either creature used dimension door. **Blinding Beams** **Trigger** The bastion archon hits a creature with two fist Strikes this turn and can use its Holy Beam; **Effect** The bastion archon fires its Holy Beam at only the creature it hit twice. The creature's save result is one degree of success worse than the result it rolled. **Entrench** The bastion archon locks itself in place, becoming voluntarily [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24). While Entrenched, the archon can't be forcibly moved or tripped, and it gains a +2 circumstance bonus to AC and Reflex saves. Entrench ends automatically as soon as the bastion archon uses another [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=114) action. **Holy Beam** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Good](/Traits.aspx?ID=83), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Light](/Traits.aspx?ID=100)) The bastion archon releases a blinding beam of holy light in a 500-foot line that deals 20d6 good damage to non-archons in the area, with a DC 38 Reflex save. The bastion archon can't use Holy Beam again for 1d4 rounds.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes half damage.
**Failure** The creature takes full damage and is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1) for 1d4 rounds.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes double damage and is blinded permanently.
name: Bastion Archon

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Assassin Vine](/Monsters.aspx?ID=555)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The assassin vine is a carnivorous plant with a voracious appetite. Rather than consuming creatures directly, it creates its own fertilizer by entangling prey and squeezing the life from it. Once the plant has killed a creature, it pulls the carcass over to its roots to feed from the decaying flesh. Although an assassin vine lacks eyes, it can detect and track prey through a combination of using its root systems to detect vibrations through topsoil and its leaves to detect heat changes in the vicinity. Although the assassin vine has no real intelligence, its learned behaviors mimic those of cunning ambush hunters.

As it is slow moving, the assassin vine typically remains rooted in a single area for as long as it has sufficient prey. Though most often found along woodland trails or swamps, these plants can also be encountered in more rural settlements, poorly tended fields, and vineyards. While assassin vines can't be trained in the classical sense, they can be used as defenses, and woodland-dwelling creatures like goblins and fey have been known to cultivate assassin vines as guardians for less-used routes into their lairs. In such cases, the tenders often seek to make the path more obvious than its disuse would otherwise suggest, while simultaneously working to ensure the bones of the vines' victims are removed to keep their presence a secret, resulting in what appears to be an easy approach to the den but is actually an organic ambush.

Mature assassin vines grow to 20 feet long and have smaller vines extending off the main trunk that reach from to 5 to 10 feet in length. The smallest of these vines sprout every 6 inches or so; they tend to grow clusters of full leaves and occasionally bear small berries. A recently fed assassin vine can have bunches of plump, blood-red berries that are juicy and tart but leave a slightly unpleasant iron aftertaste, as if from the faint taste of blood from a bitten lip.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Plant](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 18

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/AssassinVine.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 3">[Assassin Vine](/Monsters.aspx?ID=555)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="N" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Large" />
