Calathgars are mysterious predatory plants that grow in the depths of the densest frozen forests, realms where icicles rarely melt from snow-cloaked trees. A calathgar's blue flowers grow from 3 to 5 feet across (with specimens as wide as 10 feet reported), while the plant itself stands just under 4 feet in height and weighs up to 50 pounds when heavy with seeds. Calathgars' psyches are alien to most sapient creatures. They have little interest in emotion, culture, or ambition, yet they have nearly flawless memories and can share experiences with other calathgars by scent (perceptible to other creatures as a vinegary odor that may reveal their presence) as easily as humanoids can pass on information by speech. While calathgars themselves can't speak, they can typically be reasoned with—though like most protective parents, an incensed calathgar defending its young is rarely in a mood to listen. **[Recall Knowledge - Plant](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 19 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14 [Calathgar](/Monsters.aspx?ID=576) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 45 **Perception** +10; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [scent 30 feet](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) **Languages** [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10); can't speak **Skills** [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +13, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +10 **Str** +2 **Dex** +5 **Con** +2 **Int** -2 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +2 --- **AC** 21 **Fort** +10 **Ref** +13 **Will** +10 **HP** 75 **Immunities** cold **Weaknesses** fire 5, slashing 5 **Cold Healing** Calathgars are healed by cold. Anytime a calathgar would take cold damage, it instead regains 1d6 Hit Points (regardless of the amount of damage the cold effect would have caused). In [severe cold or colder environments](/Rules.aspx?ID=642), calathgars gain [fast healing 1](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=15).
**Mold Mulch** When a calathgar is reduced to 0 Hit Points, it immediately decays and dies, transforming into a 5-foot patch of mold (or a 10-foot patch of mold if it was killed by fire damage). This patch of mold persists for 1 minute, during which time it deals 3d6 cold damage to any creature that begins its turn in this area, or 1d6 cold damage to any creature that begins its turn in an adjacent square. The mold patch decays away after an hour, but it can be destroyed before then (treat each 5-foot square as an object with Hardness 0, 10 Hit Points, BT 5, immunity to cold, piercing, and slashing damage; the mold deals half its regular cold damage once it's broken). --- **Speed** 20 feet, climb 20 feet **Melee** flower +13 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)), **Damage** 2d6+4 bludgeoning plus 1d6 cold **Melee** tendril +13 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)), **Damage** 2d4+4 slashing plus 1d4 cold **Seed Spray** ([Cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) The calathgar expels thorny, frozen seeds in a 15-foot cone, dealing 1d6 piercing and 4d6 cold damage (DC 20 basic Reflex save). In [mild cold or colder environments](/Rules.aspx?ID=642), these seeds cling to living creatures they strike, dealing 1d4 [persistent cold damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). The calathgar can't use Seed Spray again for 1d4 rounds.
Vengeful Flowers Calathgar cuttings are greatly desired in warmer lands, yet horticulturalists often fail to realize that calathgars are sapient, mobile, and vengeful against those who prune their leaves or take cuttings. These feral flowers understand the speech of forest denizens and often shift and move their groves when they sense danger approaching. They protect their immature and immobile offspring, and if they discover a garden of their young has been despoiled, they track down the perpetrators to unleash a flurry of vengeance upon them.
name: Calathgar

markdown:
Caligni slayers possess greater occult talents than others of their kind. In most other calignis, the power bartered from the long-lost demigods known as the Forsaken merely burns within. But in caligni slayers, this power is a deep and terrible hunger. Slayers embrace their evil impulses as a result, and they seek to feed on others with their soul harvest ability to keep this supernatural hunger sated. Slayers denied the opportunity to feed descend into paranoia and, eventually, murderous rage. Delayed feeding improves but never fully restores their composure and patience. Perhaps the greatest manifestation of a slayer's hunger appears at the moment of their death. Upon its last breath, a slayer collapses into itself in a violent, lightless implosion, rather than the blinding eruption of other calignis' deaths, consuming the body itself in a final attempt at satiation. Slayers view themselves as the cleverest and most gifted of the calignis. They seethe with ill-concealed envy at the position of power [caligni stalkers](/Monsters.aspx?ID=66) occupy, ever scheming to displace them and claim that power for their own. For their part, stalkers tolerate these machinations as an acceptable price for the talents slayers contribute to an enclave. If necessary, any slayer who grows too ambitious can become a brave sacrifice in battle, for the good of the enclave. In physical stature, slayers fall between the willowy [caligni dancers](/Monsters.aspx?ID=64) and the diminutive [creepers](/Monsters.aspx?ID=65). Stalkers tower above most slayers—yet another source of slayers' envy and resentment. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 20 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15 [Caligni Slayer](/Monsters.aspx?ID=577) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 46 **Perception** +8; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Caligni](/Languages.aspx?ID=28), [Undercommon](/Languages.aspx?ID=11) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +9, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +10, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +7, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +10, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +10 **Str** +1 **Dex** +4 **Con** +1 **Int** +2 **Wis** +0 **Cha** +2 **[Light Blindness](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=22)** **Items** [Black Smear Poison](/Equipment.aspx?ID=485) (2 doses; see below), [Kukri](/Weapons.aspx?ID=56) --- **AC** 19 **Fort** +9 **Ref** +12 **Will** +6 **HP** 45 (death implosion) **Death Implosion** ([sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=147)) When the caligni slayer dies, their body implodes violently into nothingness, dealing 3d10 sonic damage to creatures in a 10-foot burst. Each creature in the area must attempt a DC 20 Fortitude save. The slayer's gear and treasure are unaffected by the implosion and are left in a pile where they died.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes half damage.
**Failure** The creature takes full damage and is [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=8) for 1 minute.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes double damage and is deafened for 24 hours. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** kukri +11 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=196)), **Damage** 1d6+3 slashing plus black smear poison **Occult Innate Spells** DC 20, attack +12 - **Cantrips (2nd)** [Chill Touch](/Spells.aspx?ID=35), [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=61), [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66), [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=280) - **2nd** [Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=59) (at will), [Death Knell](/Spells.aspx?ID=63), [Phantom Pain](/Spells.aspx?ID=220), [Spectral Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=295) **Black Smear Poison** ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126)) **Saving Throw** DC 16 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 1d6 poison damage and [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) (1 round); **Stage 2** As stage 1; **Stage 3** 1d6 poison damage and enfeebled 2 (1 round). See [here](/Equipment.aspx?ID=485) for full details on this alchemical poison. **Call to Blood** ([Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) Each caligni within 30 feet gains a +2 status bonus to attack rolls against [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) creatures. This bonus lasts for 1 minute. **Soul Harvest** ([Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) The caligni slayer deals an additional 2d6 negative damage to [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) creatures.
Caligni Slayer Trinkets Slayers obsess over magical trinkets, coveting them above all else. They often have a [consumable](/Traits.aspx?ID=36) or permanent item of use in combat, such as a [scroll](/Traits.aspx?ID=140), [wand](/Traits.aspx?ID=164), or [talisman](/Traits.aspx?ID=155). Unfortunately, their incessant tinkering and fiddling often leaves their treasures broken or depleted.
name: Caligni Slayer

Never have legend and misinformation met upon a more inauspicious brow than that of the lowly carbuncle. At first glance, carbuncles appear to be little more than ungainly reptiles. What sets them apart is their strange magical abilities and the gemstone-like horn protruding from between their goggling eyes. Although rumors suggest various uses for carbuncle horns, ranging from miracle cure-alls to potent magical components, the truth is much more mundane: a carbuncle's horn is merely a highly reflective growth, not unlike a fingernail. **[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 20 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15 [Carbuncle](/Monsters.aspx?ID=578) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 47 **Perception** +7; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** carbuncle empathy 30 feet **Skills** [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +3, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +6 **Str** -3 **Dex** +0 **Con** +3 **Int** -2 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +0 **Carbuncle Empathy** The carbuncle can [telepathically](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) send mild feelings and sensations to nearby creatures. It can't use this ability to communicate in language or hinder a target, but it might convey a feeling of dread or the scent of food cooking nearby. --- **AC** 16 **Fort** +8 **Ref** +3 **Will** +6 **HP** 20 **Easy to Influence** Any [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) spell can affect a carbuncle, regardless of creature type limitations. Against a [_suggestion_](/Spells.aspx?ID=315) spell, a carbuncle always gets an outcome one degree of success worse than it rolled on its saving throw.
**Fatal Faker** ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33), [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=156)) **Trigger** The carbuncle takes damage; **Effect** The carbuncle feigns death by teleporting away and leaving a replica of its corpse behind, creating a colorful flash of light and a croaking sound. The real carbuncle transports to a clear space within 30 feet that it can see, and a hollow shell remains behind. The fake body appears solid until it is touched, at which point it crumbles to dust. --- **Speed** 15 feet **Melee** jaws +5 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)), **Damage** 1d6 piercing **Primal Innate Spells** DC 18 - **Cantrips (1st)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=61) - **1st** [Jump](/Spells.aspx?ID=167) (at will) - **3rd** [Levitate](/Spells.aspx?ID=170) (at will; self only) **Specious Suggestion** ([Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) **Frequency** three times per day; **Effect** The carbuncle concentrates on a creature it can see and tries to manipulate that creature. The target must attempt a DC 18 Will save. The target then becomes temporarily immune for 24 hours.
**Critical Success** The attempt backfires and bolsters the target's mind instead, granting it a +1 status bonus to Will saving throws for 1 hour.
**Success** The target briefly experiences an unusual but harmless sensation like an unexpected flavor or scent, an urge to eat something strange, or an amusing half-forgotten memory.
**Failure** The target is compelled to spend all of its actions on its next turn performing harmless, pointless, and usually embarrassing actions.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the compulsion persists for 1 minute. The target can attempt a new save at the end of its turn each round to end the effect.
Carbuncle Chatter “A lizard with an apple-sized gem sticking out of its forehead? Wishful thinking!” “Carbuncles are real! I almost caught one, but even though it could barely walk, its magic allowed it to escape my clutches.” “Their mind control powers could make them useful familiars, but if you ever catch one, it dies of fright.” “My advice is to stay away from these pests. They drink your hopes and aspirations through their horns, leaving you empty except for bad luck and bellyaches.” “Planning a carbuncle hunt? You'd better leave your self-respect at home, and be ready to emerge with fewer friends than you had at the start!”
name: Carbuncle

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Catoblepas](/Monsters.aspx?ID=579)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The catoblepas is an aggressive beast at the best of times. Though it prefers swamps, the catoblepas has been know to forage in plains and forests for short periods, leaving behind hunting grounds tainted by its foul breath and noxious waste that other predators and prey alike avoid for days or even weeks thereafter. The catoblepas bullies those creatures it believes are a match for it, and eats everything weaker.

A catoblepas is 15 feet long and weighs 2,200 pounds.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 30

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Catoblepas.png" />
</column>

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

**Stench** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [olfactory](/Traits.aspx?ID=246)) 30 feet. A creature entering the aura or starting its turn in the aura must succeed at a DC 30 Fortitude save or become [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) (plus [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) for as long as it's sickened on a critical failure). While within the aura, affected creatures take a –2 circumstance penalty to saves against [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46) and to recover from the sickened condition. A creature that succeeds at its save is temporarily immune for 1 minute.

</column>

---

<actions string="Single Action" />

**Breath Weapon** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) The catoblepas breathes a 60-foot cone of horrid fumes, dealing 13d6 poison damage (DC 32 basic Fortitude save). The area of this cone is reduced to 30 feet underwater. Targets that fail their saving throw also become [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) (sickened 2 on a critical failure). The catoblepas can't use its Breath Weapon again for 1d4 rounds.

**[Trample](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=39)** <actions string="Three Actions" />  Medium or smaller, hoof, DC 32

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_3_Locations.png">Catoblepas Nests</title>

A catoblepas den is an awful place indeed - a filthy nest composed of piles of decaying vegetation, half-finished or half-digested meals of decaying animals, never-quite-dry banks of mud, and tangles of thorny branches. Worse, the catoblepas's stink infuses such a site, imposing the beast's ungodly stench upon those who would explore and search within for treasure. A catoblepas nest can retain its stink in this way for up to a week after it's been abandoned by its foul denizen.
</aside>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Cave Fisher](/Monsters.aspx?ID=580)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Cave fishers lurk in dark corners near the ceilings of caves and underground structures, lying in wait for prey to approach. When it does, the cave fisher excretes thin, tough, and very sticky filaments at its prey with lightning speed to capture it.

A cave fisher eats just about any live prey smaller than itself, though anything smaller than a rat isn't much of a meal for it. When a cave fisher captures such a creature, it often leaves the hapless animal entangled, using it as bait to attract larger creatures. Because the adhesive on its filament doesn't last long, the fisher frequently needs to eat one filament and excrete a new one. The filaments are translucent and can be hard to see when they're still and in darkness.

Cave fishers hatch with the ability to excrete filaments, but they molt many times before reaching their adult size of about 7 feet long and 400 pounds. They often leave shed shells in conspicuous locations as decoys.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 16

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 11
</column>

</column>

<image src="/images/Monsters/CaveFisher.png" />
</column>

</row>

**Cha** -3
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 18 

**Fort** +9 

**Ref** +9 

**Will** +7 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 30

</row>

</column>

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 15 feet, climb 15 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +10,
**Damage** 1d10+6 slashing

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
filament +11 ([range 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)),
**Damage** sticky filament

**Pull Filament** <actions string="Single Action" />  The cave fisher pulls a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) by its filament toward itself, even suspending the target vertically if necessary. The cave fisher attempts an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check against the creature's Fortitude DC. On a success, the cave fisher pulls the creature 15 feet closer to it (30 feet closer on a critical success).

**Sticky Filament**   Any creature hit by the cave fisher's sticky filament is [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20). The cave fisher can move while it has a creature grabbed with its filament, but it automatically releases the creature if the cave fisher moves beyond the filament's 60-foot length. The cave fisher can use only one filament at a time, and it can sever the filament and release any creature grabbed by it as a free action. The filament can be severed by a Strike that deals at least 10 slashing damage to it. This doesn't deal any damage to the cave fisher itself. The filament has AC 17, and its [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) DC is 19.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_5_TreasureAndRewards.png">Fisher Filaments</title>

A cave fisher can rapidly produce a cord from a gland at the back of its throat. This filament resembles a semi-transparent [silk rope](/Equipment.aspx?ID=44) and is coated with an adhesive that traps prey that comes in contact with it. When reeling in its prey, the cave fisher consumes the filament as well, slurping it up like a noodle.

 A severed filament can be harvested for use as rope. The adhesive dries after about 10 minutes. A harvested filament gland can't produce new filaments, but it can be used as 25 gp worth of raw materials for creating potent adhesives, such as sovereign glue.
</aside>
name: Cave Fisher

markdown:
With long, cartilaginous limbs and gray skin, chokers easily hide in stony alcoves, rocky fissures, and darkened staircases to ambush their prey. The strange little aberrations prefer to pick off weak and solitary creatures, especially those that wander off from their packs or communities. A choker's long, spongy arms are flexible but deceptively strong. The fingers can tighten rapidly and are ridged with spiky, tooth-like structures that provide an incredible grip. A choker typically strangles its prey to death, then drags the body off or dismembers it using crude tools if the body is too big to carry. If confronted or outnumbered, a choker tries to escape, often compressing into a tight passage to get away. Chokers that encounter humanoids exhibit intense curiosity about their culture, society, and the products of art and industry. This interest isn't very deep—it just results in chokers that like killing people and collecting any of their items that seem sophisticated, such as jewelry, nice clothing, or written texts. Chokers sometimes gather in settlements, usually sticking to slums or abandoned structures. These chokers stalk at night, flopping across rooftops, tapping at windows, and scuttling through sewers, gutters, and chimneys to get at prey. An urban choker that finds prey early on its hunt—a wandering pet, a person who is out alone—might spend the rest of the night indulging its curiosity about the products of society. A curious choker might abscond with all sorts of odd items, from tavern signs to library books to roof tiles. A choker usually pilfers only one item at a time, but as its collection grows, it might need to find a larger nest to accommodate its collection, then move its pieces one by one to the new home. **[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 16 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 11 [Choker](/Monsters.aspx?ID=583) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 51 **Perception** +7; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Undercommon](/Languages.aspx?ID=11) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +9, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +9 **Str** +3 **Dex** +3 **Con** +1 **Int** -3 **Wis** +1 **Cha** -2 --- **AC** 18 **Fort** +7 **Ref** +10 **Will** +7 **HP** 28 **Yank** ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Trigger** An enemy targets the choker with an attack; **Effect** The choker tries to pull a creature it has [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33) into the path of the attack. The choker attempts an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check, including its bonus to [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=35). If it succeeds against the grabbed or restrained creature's Fortitude DC, the choker redirects the attack to that creature, and the attacker compares its attack roll result against the new target's AC.
The choker must move the creature into a space adjacent to itself. The creature must also be moved within the attacker's reach against a melee attack or into a space between itself and the attacker against a ranged attack. The choker can't use this ability to make the attacker target itself, even if it's grabbing or restraining the attacker. --- **Speed** 20 feet, climb 15 feet **Melee** arm +11 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 1d6+3 bludgeoning plus strangling fingers **[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=10)** 1d6+3 bludgeoning, DC 19 **Hidden Movement** If the choker starts its turn hidden from or undetected by a creature, that creature is [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) against the choker's attacks until the end of the turn. **Strangling Fingers** Any creature hit by a choker's arm Strike is automatically [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20), and the choker begins to strangle the target. The creature is suffocating and can't speak as long as it's strangled. This prevents it from casting spells with a verbal component or activating items with a command component.
Choker Trinkets A choker's nest is small and confined, as the choker prefers. It doesn't tidy its nest, so everything from animal bones to valuable treasures might be within, compacted into one mass. A choker in the wilderness might have a few remnants of society taken from victims who wandered through. These remnants can include candles, maps, or animal traps. Chokers in cities have stolen goods appropriate to their location: books and [scrolls](/Traits.aspx?ID=140) if a library or wizard's college is nearby, steins and pillows near a tavern, or fishhooks and shipping manifests near a dock, for example. Chokers almost never have edible or drinkable items, as they prefer to consume these “delicacies” themselves—even [magical potions](/Equipment.aspx?Category=15&Subcategory=18) and [alchemical items](/Equipment.aspx?Category=6).
name: Choker

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Chupacabra](/Monsters.aspx?ID=584)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

These notorious predators have an undeniable thirst for blood. Chupacabras prefer to prey upon the weak and slow, often hiding in wait and watching potential prey for long periods before attacking. Spry and stealthy, they most often make their homes in areas of high grass and protective rock, their slightly reflective scales allowing them to blend in well with such surroundings.

Chupacabras prefer to eat lone travelers and farm animals (particularly goats), and leave little evidence of their presence apart from the grisly, blood-drained husks of their meals. Their tendency to stay out of sight combined with their naturally nocturnal activity often leads superstitious locals to conclude the worst, imagining that a particularly reckless vampire lives in the area.

A typical chupacabra measures nearly 5-1/2 feet from its muzzle to the tip of its spiny tail, and it stands just under 4 feet tall. With their slight build and lightweight bones, most weigh close to 100 pounds. They mate rarely and only during the hottest months, with the females each producing a single egg that hatches into a tiny, dehydrated creature. The mother typically leaves helpless prey in her cave so the hatchling can immediately feed.

Although chupacabras are typically solitary creatures, they have been known to form small gangs in bountiful areas. Members of these groups work well together, growing bold enough to attack larger animals, small herds, and otherwise more dangerous prey. Stories of chupacabras attacking travelers or laying siege to farmhouses typically stem from the hunting practices of such gangs. Regions where chupacabra activity like this is more common often have complex and colorful myths and tall tales about chupacabra capabilities or motive —- and a few of the claims, such as that some chupacabras can fly, are all too true.

**Chupar** <actions string="Single Action" />  **Requirements** The chupacabra has a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20); **Effect** The chupacabra sucks blood from the grabbed creature. The chupacabra gains the [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=32) condition for 1 minute and can use the extra action only for Strike and Stride actions. A chupacabra can't use Chupar again while it is quickened in this way. A creature that has its blood drained by a chupacabra is [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) until it receives healing (of any kind or amount).

**Pounce** <actions string="Single Action" />  The chupacabra Strides and makes a Strike at the end of that movement. If the chupacabra began this action [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22), it remains hidden until after this ability's Strike.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="">Winged Chupacabras</title>

Some chupacabras are mutants with large reptilian wings and have been known to carry off goats or even children. A winged chupacabra has a fly Speed of 50 feet. Other chupacabras grow much larger, up to Medium sized, and can stand eye to eye with a full-grown human. These chupacabras have [elite adjustments](/Rules.aspx?ID=790) to their statistics and deal 2d6+5 damage with their jaws Strikes.
</aside>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Cockroach Swarm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=586)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

While cockroaches tend to gather in cramped spaces, a disturbed colony is sometimes prone to swarming, where hundreds or even thousands of the insects scurry out of their hiding places in a raft of shiny brown and black carapaces on thousands of skittering legs. Given how disgusting many find cockroaches to be, encountering such a swarm can be alarming to even the most seasoned adventurers. The creatures are relentless when disturbed; in contrast to the insects' normally skittish and harmless nature, as swarms they pursue the creature or creatures that provoked them and harry them with thousands of stinging bites.

These sacred feathered serpents tirelessly serve the powers of law and good. Some operate directly as messengers and intermediaries of the deities, while others operate independently in aiding the cause of righteousness. Either way, they watch over mortals and try to influence and aid them from the shadows, shifting from plane to plane to spread wisdom and healing where they are needed. Some quetz couatls are worshipped as divinities in remote or isolated societies, and while they do not encourage such veneration, they use the trust placed in them to foster peace and cooperation with others. Quetz couatls are typically 10 to 20 feet long, with iridescent blue and green scales. Their glorious wings of rainbow-hued feathers span 15 feet and weigh nearly a ton. They are carnivorous, feeding on birds, mammals, and even the occasional evil [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=91). **[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 29 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 [Quetz Couatl](/Monsters.aspx?ID=587) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 54 **Perception** +21; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [_detect alignment_](/Spells.aspx?ID=65) **Languages** [Celestial](/Languages.aspx?ID=16), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2); [telepathy 100 feet](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +16, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +19, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +22, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +22, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +19, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +22, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +16 **Str** +7 **Dex** +3 **Con** +5 **Int** +6 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +5 --- **AC** 30 **Fort** +19 **Ref** +19 **Will** +21 **HP** 175 --- **Speed** 15 feet, fly 50 feet **Melee** jaws +23 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 2d10+13 piercing plus quetz couatl venom and [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Divine Innate Spells** DC 29 - **Cantrips (5th)** [Disrupt Undead](/Spells.aspx?ID=79), [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=171), [Mage Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=177) - **2nd** [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=164) (self only; at will) - **3rd** [Mind Reading](/Spells.aspx?ID=194) (at will) - **4th** [Charm](/Spells.aspx?ID=34), [Gaseous Form](/Spells.aspx?ID=129) - **5th** [Breath of Life](/Spells.aspx?ID=29), [Divine Wrath](/Spells.aspx?ID=86) - **7th** [Ethereal Jaunt](/Spells.aspx?ID=105) (at will), [Plane Shift](/Spells.aspx?ID=222) (self only) - **Constant (5th)** [Detect Alignment](/Spells.aspx?ID=65) (all alignments simultaneously) **[Greater Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=19)** 2d10+7 bludgeoning, DC 29 **Quetz Couatl Venom** ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126)) A quetz couatl's venom deals good damage rather than poison damage to fiends; **Saving Throw** DC 29 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 2d6 poison damage and [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) (1 round); **Stage 2** 2d8 poison damage, enfeebled 1, and [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) (1 round); **Stage 3** 2d10 poison damage, enfeebled 2, and flat-footed (1 round) **Radiant Wings** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)) The quetz couatl spreads its multicolored wings and radiant plumage. Each enemy within 30 feet must attempt a DC 29 Will save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected and is temporarily immune to Radiant Wings for 24 hours.
**Success** The creature is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) for 1 round.
**Failure** The creature is dazzled for 1 minute.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but if the creature is evil, it is also [stunned 3](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36). **Wrap in Coils** **Requirements** The quetz couatl has a Medium or smaller creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33) in its jaws; **Effect** The quetz couatl moves the creature into its coils, freeing its fangs to make attacks, then uses [Greater Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=19) against the creature. The quetz couatl can hold as many creatures in its coils as will fit in its space.
name: Quetz Couatl

Crabs are scavenging crustaceans known for their hard shells and iconic sideways gait. They use their claws to defend themselves, hunt, and fight other crabs for territory. When confronted with threats from outside their species, most crabs prefer to flee, but when retreat isn't possible, they clamp on to their foes as tightly as they can. The statistics presented here represent giant crabs that live close to the water's surface. Crabs that live deeper underwater often exhibit more extreme adaptations to their environment. Crabs who live in the depths where little light reaches gain darkvision and cold resistance, and those adapted to the most hostile reaches of the deep sea can detect nearby creatures through subtle shifts in ocean currents. These skittering creatures are prized for their delicious meat, but their size makes them dangerous targets for harvesting. **[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 16 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 11 [Giant Crab](/Monsters.aspx?ID=588) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 55 **Perception** +8; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +7, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +8, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +7 **Str** +4 **Dex** +3 **Con** +1 **Int** -4 **Wis** +2 **Cha** -3 --- **AC** 19 **Fort** +7 **Ref** +9 **Will** +8 **HP** 24 **Resistances** physical 3 (except bludgeoning) **Vulnerable to [Prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31)** If a creature critically succeeds on a check to [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=40) the giant crab, the crab is flipped over onto its back in addition to the usual effects. A giant crab that is flipped onto its back has a particularly hard time defending itself; instead of taking the normal –2 circumstance penalty to AC for being [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16), it takes a –4 circumstance penalty to AC.
**Scuttle** **Trigger** A creature that the giant crab can see targets the crab with an attack; **Effect** The giant crab scuttles to the side and gains a +2 circumstance bonus to AC against the triggering attack. After the attack resolves, the crab can Stride up to its speed in a straight line as part of the reaction. --- **Speed** 25 feet, swim 15 feet **Melee** claw +10, **Damage** 1d10+4 slashing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=10)** 1d6+4 bludgeoning, DC 18
name: Giant Crab

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Crawling Hand](/Monsters.aspx?ID=589)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

A crawling hand formed from the appendage of a Medium creature is quick and agile, skittering in the shadows until it can strike its prey.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 13

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 11

</column>

</column>

</column>

<traits>

<trait label="Tiny" />
<trait label="Undead" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=160" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 56

**Perception** +5; [lifesense 30 feet](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=21), [tremorsense (imprecise) 30 feet](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=40)

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1); can't speak any language

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +5, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +6, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +2

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +1

**Dex** +3

**Con** +0

**Int** -4

**Wis** +0

**Cha** +0
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 12 

**Fort** +2 

**Ref** +5 

**Will** +2 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 8
([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42))

</row>

**Immunities**
[death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 30 feet, climb 30 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +7 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)),
**Damage** 1d4+1 slashing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18)

**Grip Throat**   A Medium or smaller creature that is [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) by the crawling hand has difficulty speaking and must spend an extra action to perform any action with the verbal trait.

**Mark Quarry**   A crawling hand can be assigned a quarry by anointing the hand with a drop of the intended quarry's blood. If the hand ever has no quarry, it automatically gains the next creature it damages as its quarry. The hand gains a +1 circumstance bonus to Perception checks when it [Seeks](/Actions.aspx?ID=84) its quarry, to [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) checks when it [Tracks](/Actions.aspx?ID=66) its quarry, and damage rolls when it Strikes its quarry.

</column>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-130" />
name: Crawling Hand

The affected creature must attempt a DC 21 Reflex save.

If the hand ever has no quarry, it automatically gains the next creature it damages as its quarry. The hand gains a +1 circumstance bonus to Perception checks when it [Seeks](/Actions.aspx?ID=84) its quarry, to [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) checks when it [Tracks](/Actions.aspx?ID=66) its quarry, and damage rolls when it Strikes its quarry.

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Culdewen](/Monsters.aspx?ID=591)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Perpetually cursed to hunger and failure, a culdewen plies waterways in search of appealing fish to catch—the bigger the better. Of course, this diminutive, emaciated fey calls every living creature a “fish.” Only other fey are exempt, and they tend to consider culdewens lost causes. A culdewen can never find satisfaction. Each time they catch a fish, a magical curse forces them to hunt for an appealing spot to dine—an island, cove, or abandoned dock or shack where they can eat in peace. By the time they arrive, their catch will be gone. Every time. Forever.

A foul mood hovers over a culdewen, causing them to swear colorfully, smack things with their oar, and shake their tiny fists at the sky. Conversation with a culdewen is fruitless, as they talk in circles, speaking only of fishing and the weather. Capturing a “fish” gives a culdewen a rare moment of happiness; the culdewen capers and ululates, pleased with their catch, and rushes off to their boat.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 25

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Culdewen.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 7">[Culdewen](/Monsters.aspx?ID=591)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="CE" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Small" />
<trait label="Amphibious" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=207" />
<trait label="Fey" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=69" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 57

**Perception** +15; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=23)

**Languages**
[Aquan](/Languages.aspx?ID=14), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10)

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +16, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +16, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +14, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +12, [Sailing Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +14, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +15, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +16

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +4

**Dex** +5

**Con** +1

**Int** -1

**Wis** +2

**Cha** +4
</row>

**Culdewen's Curse** ([curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) A culdewen that captures a creature is compelled to immediately retreat to a secluded place to eat, typically journeying by boat. The travel to the secluded place always takes at least 1 day. Their captive is cursed to vanish on the journey—typically being lost in the water or consumed by aquatic creatures—and can be recovered only by a [_wish_](/Spells.aspx?ID=377) spell or similarly powerful magic.

**Items**
fish hook, oar (functions as [mace](/Weapons.aspx?ID=7))

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 25 

**Fort** +14 

**Ref** +18 

**Will** +13 

–2 status to all saves vs. curses
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 105

</row>

**Weaknesses**
[cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=272) 5

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 40 feet, swim 40 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fish hook +17 ([Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=196)),
**Damage** 2d10+7 piercing plus hooked

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
oar +17 ([Shove](/Traits.aspx?ID=193)),
**Damage** 2d6+7 bludgeoning

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fish hook +18 ([thrown 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=195)),
**Damage** 2d10+7 piercing plus hooked

**Hooked**   A creature struck by the culdewen's fish hook is skewered, taking 1d4 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) as long as the hook remains stuck in it. The fish hook can be removed if a creature [Escapes](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) (DC 25), pulling it free. 

**Land the Fish** <actions string="Single Action" />  **Requirements** A creature is hooked by the culdewen's fish hook and adjacent to the culdewen; **Effect** The culdewen hoists the creature over their shoulder, as though it weighs no more than a fish. Unless the creature is unable to act, the culdewen must attempt an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check against the creature's Fortitude DC. On a success, the creature is [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33) by the culdewen. [Escaping](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) from the restrained condition (DC 25) also allows a creature to remove the hook. While hoisting a creature, the culdewen gains a +20-foot status bonus to their Speeds and is compelled to escape with it (see culdewen's curse above).

**Reel In** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) The culdewen pulls the rope attached to their hook. If they have no creature hooked, the fish hook returns to the culdewen's hand. If a creature is hooked, the culdewen attempts an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check against the creature's Fortitude DC, pulling it up to 30 feet closer on a success (60 feet on a critical success).

**Swear** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) The culdewen unleashes a string of invectives. Each creature that hears them must attempt a DC 25 Will save, with the effects of [_mariner's curse_](/Spells.aspx?ID=184) and is then temporarily immune for 24 hours. Even on a failed save, the curse ends automatically after 1 day.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">The Curse of Crawley Jack</title>

The damnable curse of the culdewen traces back to the [First World](/Planes.aspx?ID=10), when a bitter old culdewen named Crawley Jack poached a fish from the pool of a powerful fey noble. Short of temper and feeling vindictive, the noble doomed Crawley Jack's people to never consume another fish again. Yet their desire to eat fish never diminished. The curse's punishment is exacted in endless creative ways. A culdewen's catch might be rescued by [merfolk](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=73), stolen by [skum](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3), eaten by nipping sharks, or carried away on a piece of driftwood when the culdewen's boat is destroyed in a storm or shipwreck.
</aside>
name: Culdewen

Scions of death by poisoning, piscodaemons are cruel even by daemonic standards, delighting in slow and painful suffering. To a piscodaemon, death is but the icing on a putrescent cake—its true pleasure comes from watching, hearing, smelling, and even tasting raw anguish. While poisons and venoms are piscodaemons' preferred tools, their cruel claws are more than capable of tearing enemies asunder if necessary. Piscodaemons dwell in fetid swamps and noxious waterways, including the River Styx, the Bile Sluice, and the Drowning Court of Charon, Horseman of Death. They are often accompanied by cadres of hydrodaemons—representations of death by drowning. While they are cunning commanders, piscodaemons are apt to forget their station in the heat of battle and slice their way to the center of the fray. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 27 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 [Piscodaemon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=592) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 58 **Perception** +19; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [_see invisibility_](/Spells.aspx?ID=271) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Daemonic](/Languages.aspx?ID=31); [telepathy 100 feet](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +22, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +19, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) +17, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +22, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +19 **Str** +6 **Dex** +4 **Con** +6 **Int** +2 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +3 --- **AC** 28 **Fort** +22 **Ref** +16 **Will** +19 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 200 **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126) **Weaknesses** good 10 **[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)**
**Enhance Venom** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=110), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126)) **Trigger** A creature within 30 feet attempts a saving throw against piscovenom; **Effect** The creature takes an additional 2d8 poison damage even if it succeeds at its save. --- **Speed** 25 feet, swim 40 feet **Melee** claw +23 ([Evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 2d10+12 slashing plus 1d6 evil and [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Melee** tentacle +23 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 2d6+12 bludgeoning plus 1d6 evil and piscovenom **Divine Innate Spells** DC 29 - **1st** [Detect Alignment](/Spells.aspx?ID=65) (at will; good only), [Detect Poison](/Spells.aspx?ID=67) (at will) - **4th** [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) (at will), [Stinking Cloud](/Spells.aspx?ID=309) (×3) - **5th** [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) - **Constant (2nd)** [See Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=271) **[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=10)** 2d10+6 bludgeoning, DC 30 **Gory Rend** The piscodaemon makes two claw Strikes against the same creature. If both hit, the creature takes 2d10 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) and is exposed to piscovenom. **Piscovenom** ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126)) **Saving Throw** DC 30 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 1d8 poison and [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) (1 round); **Stage 2** 2d8 poison and enfeebled 1 (1 round); **Stage 3** 4d8 poison and enfeebled 2 (1 round)
name: Piscodaemon

Meladaemons personify death by starvation and thirst, and revel in spreading the same despair that brought about their mortal demise. When they aren't blighting fields, massacring livestock, or tainting water supplies, they experiment on prisoners to study how long creatures can go without sustenance and the deleterious effects that result from such deprivation. Fiercely loyal to Trelmarixian, Horseman of Famine, they serve no other beings. They work alongside other daemons if Trelmarixian wills it, but are notoriously traitorous. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 28 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 [Meladaemon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=593) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 58 **Perception** +21; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [lifesense (imprecise) 30 feet](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=21) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Daemonic](/Languages.aspx?ID=31); [telepathy 100 feet](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +20, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +23, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +23, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +20, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +23, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +19 **Str** +7 **Dex** +5 **Con** +6 **Int** +3 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +6 --- **AC** 31 **Fort** +23 **Ref** +20 **Will** +19 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 225 **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects **Weaknesses** good 10 **Consumptive Aura** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48)) 20 feet. A meladaemon emanates an aura of intense hunger. Each round a creature begins its turn in the aura, it must attempt a DC 27 Fortitude save. On a failure, the creature takes 1d6 negative damage (2d6 on a critical failure) and becomes [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15). This fatigue ends as soon as the creature eats any food.
**Withering Opportunity** **Trigger** The meladaemon is attacked by an adjacent creature and the attack misses; **Effect** The meladaemon swipes at the triggering creature, which must immediately attempt a save against the meladaemon's withering touch. --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 50 feet **Melee** bite +24 ([Evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d12+13 plus 1d6 evil and daemonic famine **Melee** claw +24 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d8+13 slashing plus 1d6 evil, [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18), and withering touch **Divine Innate Spells** DC 31 - **1st** [Detect Alignment](/Spells.aspx?ID=65) (good only; at will) - **4th** [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) (at will) - **5th** [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69), [Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=110), [Magic Missile](/Spells.aspx?ID=180) (at will) - **6th** [Phantom Pain](/Spells.aspx?ID=220) **Rituals** DC 31 - **4th** [Blight](/Rituals.aspx?ID=4) **Daemonic Famine** ([Disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46)) **Saving Throw** DC 29 Fortitude; **Stage 1** carrier (1 day); **Stage 2** [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) (1 day); **Stage 3** enfeebled 2 (1 day); **Stage 4** as stage 3; **Stage 5** enfeebled 3 (1 week); **Stage 6** dead **Withering Touch** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) When the meladaemon hits with a claw Strike or a creature begins its turn grabbed by the meladaemon, the creature must attempt a DC 30 Fortitude save. On a failure, the creature takes 1d6 negative damage and becomes [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15). This fatigue ends when the creature drinks.
Formed in his Image Meladaemons have always been gaunt and bestial, but they didn't always resemble jackals. When [Trelmarixian](/Deities.aspx?ID=137) overthrew the previous Horseman of Famine, one of his first acts as a ruler of Abaddon was to forcibly twist the appearance of his deacon caste to resemble his own wicked form. He went on to imbue meladaemons with other jackal-like aspects as it suited him, further warping them and cementing their fealty.
name: Meladaemon

markdown:
Derghodaemons represent death through unfathomable violence and being eaten alive. Whether in life a ravenous murderer hacked them to bits for stew, a werewolf tore them limb from limb, or they simply succumbed to the elements and became worm meat, derghodaemons seek to inflict the same twisted and bloody ends upon any they come across. Unlike some daemons, derghodaemons derive little pleasure from prolonged deaths—to these fiends, the quicker they can enact a gory demise, the sooner they can turn their attentions to their next victim and repeat the violent cycle. Derghodaemons look like walking masses of razor-sharp insect claws connected to equally menacing chitinous limbs, but few linger on their appearance for long— the gaze of a derghodaemon is enough to cause others in the vicinity to experience severe visual warping. The derghodaemon's appearance becomes more and more twisted as those who stare at it experience vicious hallucinations and ravenous urges to inflict harm on and devour others. Perhaps the most horrific attack derghodaemons can unleash upon their victims, though, is their swarming infestation—the daemons cough up a crawling, buzzing cloud of ravenous insects that cling to those targeted and chew them to death, provided the mind-numbing, half-whispered threats made by the rasp of the insects' bodies don't drive them to violent fury first! **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 30 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 [Derghodaemon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=594) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 59 **Perception** +24; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [scent (imprecise) 60 feet](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Daemonic](/Languages.aspx?ID=31); [telepathy 100 feet](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +22, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +25, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +24, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +24 **Str** +7 **Dex** +6 **Con** +5 **Int** +1 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +6 --- **AC** 33 **Fort** +21 **Ref** +24 **Will** +22 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 240 **Immunities** [confusion](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5), [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [swarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=239) attacks **Weaknesses** good 10 --- **Speed** 35 feet **Melee** claw +25 ([deadly 1d12](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d8+10 slashing plus 1d6 evil **Ranged** swarm spit +25 ([Evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [range 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 2d6+13 piercing plus 1d6 evil **Divine Innate Spells** DC 32 - **1st** [Detect Alignment](/Spells.aspx?ID=65) (good only; at will) - **4th** [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) (at will) - **5th** [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) - **6th** [Blade Barrier](/Spells.aspx?ID=24), [Feeblemind](/Spells.aspx?ID=112) (×3) **Derghodaemon's Stare** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)) A non-evil target must succeed at a DC 32 Will save or become [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5) for 1 round, or 1 minute on a critical failure. It is temporarily immune for 10 minutes. **[Rend](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=30)** claw **Savage Assault** The derghodaemon makes up to five claw Strikes, each against a different target. These attacks count toward the derghodaemon's multiple attack penalty, but the penalty doesn't increase until after all the attacks. **Swarming Infestation** The derghodaemon spews a large swarm, making a swarm spit Strike against up to two adjacent targets within range. Whether or not the targets are hit, each must attempt a DC 31 Reflex save (at a –2 circumstance penalty if it was actually hit by the swarm spit). [Persistent damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) caused by the swarming infestation ends as soon as the creature takes any amount of damage from an area effect. The derghodaemon can't use this ability for 1d4 rounds.
**Critical Success** The swarm disperses as soon as it deals its swarm spit Strike damage.
**Success** The swarm clings to the target and infests it, dealing 1d6 persistent piercing damage.
**Failure** The swarm clings to the target and infests it, dealing 2d6 persistent piercing damage.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the hissing sounds caused by the infestation also cause the target to become [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5) for 1 round; this additional effect has the [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), and [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) traits.
name: Derghodaemon

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Thanadaemon (Death Daemon)](/Monsters.aspx?ID=595)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Also known as the deacons of death, thanadaemons represent death via old age. They are among the most patient daemons, and prefer to bide their time to enact far-reaching, decades-long plans rather than fight (though they are still deadly foes). Like the Horseman of Death, they ply the waters of the River Styx in search of wayward souls. Rarely are thanadaemons seen without a trademark oar (which they wield as a [bo staff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=17)), a tool they use to navigate the river's muck-ridden channels and turbulent rapids.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 31

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Daemon_Thanadaemon.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 13">[Thanadaemon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=595)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="NE" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Daemon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=213" />
<trait label="Fiend" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=70" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 60

**Perception** +26; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [_true seeing_](/Spells.aspx?ID=344)

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Daemonic](/Languages.aspx?ID=31); [telepathy 100 feet](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37)

**Skills**
[Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +22, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +26, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +26, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +22, [Styx Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +24

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +6

**Dex** +6

**Con** +4

**Int** +3

**Wis** +5

**Cha** +7
</row>

**Items**
_+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [bo staff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=17)_, soul gem (2)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 34 

**Fort** +21 

**Ref** +23 

**Will** +26 

+1 status to all saves vs. magic
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 270

</row>

**Immunities**
[death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects

**Weaknesses**
good 10

**Terrifying Gaze** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)) 30 feet. When a creature ends its turn in the aura, it must attempt a DC 30 Will save. If the creature fails, it becomes [frightened 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19). The creature is then temporarily immune to terrifying gaze (but not Focus Gaze) for 24 hours.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet; [_air walk_](/Spells.aspx?ID=6)

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
bo staff +28 ([Evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Parry](/Traits.aspx?ID=190), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192), [Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=196)),
**Damage** 3d8+14 bludgeoning plus 1d6 negative, 1d6 evil, and draining strike

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +27 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)),
**Damage** 3d6+14 slashing plus 1d6 evil and draining strike

**Divine Innate Spells** DC 34
- **1st**
[Detect Alignment](/Spells.aspx?ID=65) (good only; at will)
- **4th**
[Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) (at will)
- **5th**
[Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69)
- **6th**
[Slow](/Spells.aspx?ID=289), [Vampiric Exsanguination](/Spells.aspx?ID=353) (×2)
- **7th**
[Finger of Death](/Spells.aspx?ID=116), [Plane Shift](/Spells.aspx?ID=222) (at will; self plus skiff and passengers only; [Astral](/Planes.aspx?ID=8), [Ethereal](/Planes.aspx?ID=9), and evil planes only), [Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=338)
- **Constant (7th)**
[Air Walk](/Spells.aspx?ID=6), [True Seeing](/Spells.aspx?ID=344)

**Rituals** DC 34

- **2nd**
[Create Undead](/Rituals.aspx?ID=10)

**Draining Strike**  ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) When a thanadaemon damages a living creature with a melee Strike, the creature must succeed at a DC 33 Fortitude save or become [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10). Further damage dealt by the thanadaemon increases the drained condition value by 1 on a failed save, to a maximum of drained 4.

**Focus Gaze** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)) The thanadaemon glares at a single creature they can see within 30 feet. If the target wasn't already [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19), they must immediately attempt a DC 33 Will save against the thanadaemon's terrifying gaze. If the target was already frightened, they must attempt a DC 33 Will save or become [fleeing](/Conditions.aspx?ID=17) for 1d4 rounds; this second effect has the [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93) trait. After attempting its save, the creature is temporarily immune to this ability until the start of the thanadaemon's next turn.

**Soul Crush** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Requirements** The thanadaemon has a soul gem; **Effect** The thanadaemon crushes the soul gem in one hand and gains [fast healing 15](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=15) for 1 minute.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Styx Passage Fees</title>

Gliding with eerie ease along the murky River Styx, thanadaemons are only too happy to offer mortals passage aboard their dilapidated skiffs—for a price. Gold is typically an acceptable payment for a thanadaemon's services, though these fiends are well-known for altering the details of arrangements after the fact, and may just as likely demand a favor or some esoteric good, such as a soul gem, instead of coin.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-26" />
name: Thanadaemon

Few creatures embody war's sheer amount of bloodshed and loss of life as deeply as the purrodaemons, deacons of war. These giant humanoid daemons are bedecked in black, unholy armor, with weapons piercing their flesh at every opening. Far from wounds, however, these weapons are the purrodaemon's arsenal. A purrodaemon can, with frightening swiftness and nauseating ease, extract a weapon from its own flesh as though drawing a sword from a sheath, and their blood is so tainted with evil that weapons steeped in it become powerful tools of war. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 38 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 36 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 33 [Purrodaemon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=596) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 61 **Perception** +33; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [_true seeing_](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Daemonic](/Languages.aspx?ID=31); [telepathy 100 feet](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +37, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +35, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +30, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +34, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +33, [Warfare Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +32 **Str** +9 **Dex** +6 **Con** +7 **Int** +4 **Wis** +7 **Cha** +7 **Items** _+2 [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [glaive](/Weapons.aspx?ID=20)_, soul gem (2), steeped weapon (7-10) --- **AC** 43 **Fort** +33 **Ref** +30 **Will** +29 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 335 **Immunities** bleed, [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects **Resistances** piercing 15 **Weaknesses** good 15 --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 50 feet **Melee** glaive +37 ([deadly d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64), [Forceful](/Traits.aspx?ID=180), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d8+17 slashing plus 2d6 evil and 4d6 [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) **Ranged** hurled weapon +35 ([deadly 1d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Propulsive](/Traits.aspx?ID=191), [range 120 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 2d10+12 piercing plus 1d6 evil and 4d6 [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) **Divine Innate Spells** DC 37 - **1st** [Detect Alignment](/Spells.aspx?ID=65) (good only; at will) - **4th** [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) (at will) - **5th** [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) - **7th** [Flame Strike](/Spells.aspx?ID=120), [Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=338) - **9th** [Bind Soul](/Spells.aspx?ID=21), [Blade Barrier](/Spells.aspx?ID=24), [Chain Lightning](/Spells.aspx?ID=33) - **Constant (6th)** [True Seeing](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) **Hurl Weapon** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65)) The purrodaemon causes a weapon that has steeped in their flesh (see Steep Weapon) to telekinetically launch from their flesh. The purrodaemon makes a hurled weapon Strike without using their hands. **Recall Weapon** ([Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65)) **Requirements** A steeped weapon that's no longer sheathed in the purrodaemon's body is within 120 feet of the daemon; **Effect** The steeped weapon swiftly flies through the air to resheath itself in the purrodaemon's body. If a creature is along this flight path, the purrodaemon can make a hurled weapon Strike against the target; if it hits, the weapon drops to the ground in a square adjacent to the creature. **Steep Weapon** ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) The purrodaemon sheathes a weapon in their own flesh. This deals no damage to the purrodaemon, which can have up to 10 weapons sheathed in their body at a time. A steeped weapon must be one that deals piercing or slashing damage.
If a purrodaemon [Interacts](/Actions.aspx?ID=501) to crush a soul gem, one weapon of their choice sheathed in their flesh becomes enchanted with the daemon's fiendish power and becomes a +2 [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) weapon that can be used in place of their glaive or hurled at targets (with a +2 item bonus to the hurled weapon's attack modifier and an extra d10 of damage). This magical quality fades 24 hours after it ceases being sheathed in the daemon's living body. A steeped weapon can be [Disarmed](/Actions.aspx?ID=41). **Twist the Blade** **Requirements** The purrodaemon has fewer than 10 weapons sheathed in their body; **Trigger** The purrodaemon is hit with a weapon that deals piercing damage; **Effect** The purrodaemon seizes the triggering weapon. The weapon's wielder must attempt a DC 40 Reflex save. On a failure, the weapon is disarmed and falls to an adjacent square. On a critical failure, the weapon is sheathed in the purrodaemon's body as though the daemon had used Steep Weapon.
name: Purrodaemon

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Olethrodaemon (Apocalypse Daemon)](/Monsters.aspx?ID=597)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Even daemons fear the most massive and terrifying of their kind, olethrodaemons. No single entity could embody the level of evil necessary to manifest one; instead, these living war machines are made from the souls of many—or so the story goes. Other theories suggest that the four Horsemen of the Apocalypse are responsible for creating the olethrodaemons, or that olethrodaemons are merely the seeping run-off of an even greater being: a mysterious fifth Horseman.

Whatever the truth, olethrodaemons are real, and their existence is terrifying enough to instill paralyzing existential dread. With dozens of unblinking glowing eyes above a drooling maw, two sets of four muscled limbs, and countless horns, olethrodaemons resemble no other creature known to mortalkind.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 40

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 38

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35
</column>

jaws +38 ([Evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 4d12+17 piercing plus 1d6 evil and [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18)

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +38 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)),
**Damage** 4d8+17 slashing plus 1d6 evil and [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18)

**Divine Innate Spells** DC 42, attack +34
- **1st**
[Detect Alignment](/Spells.aspx?ID=65) (good only; at will)
- **4th**
[Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) (at will)
- **5th**
[Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69)

**Apocalypse Breath** <actions string="Two Actions or Three Actions" /> ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Negative](/Traits.aspx?ID=118)) The daemon expels a shrieking black cloud of smoke from its mouth in a 120-foot line or a 60-foot cone (or both a line and a cone in the same direction if it spends 3 actions). Living creatures in the area take 24d6 negative damage (DC 45 basic Reflex save). The olethrodaemon can't use Apocalypse Breath again for 1d4 rounds.

**Soul Crush** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Requirement** The olethrodaemon has a soul gem; **Effect** The olethrodaemon crushes the soul gem in one hand and regains the use of Apocalypse Breath or any one of its innate spells.

**[Swallow Whole](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=35)** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=15)) Huge, 4d10+10 bludgeoning and [drained 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10), Rupture 43. An olethrodaemon has numerous stomachs connected by labyrinthine digestive organs. Swallowed creatures cannot [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79), but can attempt to cut their way out with a 1d6 roll. On a roll of 1–3, the creature cuts into yet another stomach. On a roll of 4–6, the creature manages to cut its way out of the olethrodaemon.

</column>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-26" />
name: Olethrodaemon

The babau is an assassin, a murderer, and a sadist—certainly not unusual traits, yet their penchant for stealth and surprise sets them apart from their kin. Babaus form from mortal souls of lone killers—those who took pleasure in more personal murders, and particularly those with grisly patterns to their killings. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 22 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 [Babau](/Monsters.aspx?ID=598) **Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 64 **Perception** +13; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [_see invisibility_](/Spells.aspx?ID=271) **Languages** [Abyssal](/Languages.aspx?ID=12), [Celestial](/Languages.aspx?ID=16), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2); [telepathy 100 feet](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +12, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +13, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +14, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +10, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +16, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) +14 **Str** +4 **Dex** +4 **Con** +4 **Int** +2 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +2 **Items** [_+1 longspear_](/Weapons.aspx?ID=6) --- **AC** 24 **Fort** +14 **Ref** +16 **Will** +11 +1 to all saves vs. magic **HP** 130 **Resistances** acid 10 **Weaknesses** cold iron 5, good 5 **Mercy Vulnerability** Babaus revel in gore, and when wounds are healed, they recoil in pain. When a creature heals from damage that the babau dealt on their last turn with Grievous Strike, sneak attack, or a critical hit, the demon takes 4d6 [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) damage. The babau can take this mental damage only once per round.
**Reactive Slime** **Trigger** A creature within the babau's reach successfully hits the babau with a Strike; **Effect** The babau excretes a gout of acidic, bloodlike slime against the attacker and its weapon. The attacker must attempt a DC 24 Reflex save.
**Critical Success** The attacker is unaffected.
**Success** The attacker takes 1d6 acid damage.
**Failure** The attacker takes 2d6 acid damage.
**Critical Failure** As failure, except the weapon used to Strike the babau becomes [broken](/Conditions.aspx?ID=2), unless the weapon is made of a material that is immune to acid. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** longspear +17 ([Evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 1d8+7 piercing plus 1d6 evil **Melee** jaws +16 ([Evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 1d10+7 piercing plus 1d6 evil **Melee** claw +16 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 1d4+7 slashing plus 1d6 evil **Divine Innate Spells** DC 24 - **2nd** [Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=59) - **4th** [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) (at will) - **5th** [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) - **Constant (5th)** [See Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=271) **Rituals** DC 24 - **1st** [Abyssal Pact](/Rituals.aspx?ID=21) **Grievous Strike** The babau attacks with the intent of creating a particularly horrific and gory wound. The babau makes a melee Strike. This counts as two attacks when calculating the daemon's multiple attack penalty. If this Strike hits, the babau deals an additional 2d6 damage of the same damage type, and the creature struck is [frightened 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19). **[Sneak Attack](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=34)** The babau's Strikes deal an extra 2d6 precision damage to [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) creatures.
Signs of Murder Babaus revel in their art and often leave behind a grim token or marker as a signature. They may leave a distinctive mutilation upon the bodies of their victims, rather than harvesting keepsakes and trophies for a personal collection. Babaus want their legacy to grow among those they hunt and kill.
name: Babau

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Invidiak (Shadow Demon)](/Monsters.aspx?ID=599)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

For most demons, possessing a creature requires a ritual, magic item, or condition, but for invidiaks, who lack a body, possession is akin to donning a suit of clothing. Known also as shadow demons, invidiaks are formed from envious mortal souls. 

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 23

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Demon_Invidiak.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 7">[Invidiak](/Monsters.aspx?ID=599)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="CE" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Demon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=42" />
<trait label="Fiend" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=70" />
<trait label="Incorporeal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=222" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Bestiary 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=32) pg. 65

**Perception** +15; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12)

**Languages**
[Abyssal](/Languages.aspx?ID=12), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1); [telepathy 100 feet](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +15, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +18, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +13, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +15, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +17

<row gap="medium">
**Str** -5

**Dex** +4

**Con** +3

**Int** +2

**Wis** +2

**Cha** +5
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 22 

**Fort** +15 

**Ref** +16 

**Will** +13 

+1 to all saves vs. magic
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 90

</row>

**Immunities**
[disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), precision

**Resistances**
all 5 (except [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75), [ghost touch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297), or positive; double resistance vs. non-magical)

**Weaknesses**
[cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=272) 5, good 5

**Exorcism Vulnerability** If an invidiak attempts to possess a creature and the creature rolls a critical success on its saving throw to resist [_possession_](/Spells.aspx?ID=225), or if an invidiak's possession of a creature is ended prematurely, such as via a successful dispel magic, the invidiak takes 6d6 mental damage and cannot use possession for 24 hours.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** fly 30 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +13 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)),
**Damage** 2d8 cold plus 2d6 evil

**Divine Innate Spells** DC 26
- **Cantrips (4th)**
[Telekinetic Projectile](/Spells.aspx?ID=334)
- **3rd**
[Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=110)
- **4th**
[Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=59), [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) (at will)
- **5th**
[Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69), [Shadow Blast](/Spells.aspx?ID=273)
- **7th**
[Possession](/Spells.aspx?ID=225) (range touch)

**Rituals** DC 26

- **1st**
[Abyssal Pact](/Rituals.aspx?ID=21)

**Favored Host**   Each invidiak has a particular type of creature they prefer possessing, chosen from the following list: [aberration](/Traits.aspx?ID=1), [animal](/Traits.aspx?ID=9), [beast](/Traits.aspx?ID=20), or any [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=91) (such as [dwarf](/Traits.aspx?ID=54))—in most cases, the favored type of target is [human](/Traits.aspx?ID=90). When an invidiak uses possession against a favored host, the duration of possession increases to unlimited, and the effect lasts until dispelled or until the invidiak dismisses it.

**Shadow Blend** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=92)) The invidiak Strides up to twice their speed. If the invidiak ends their movement in an area of dim light, they become hidden as long as they don't move and the lighting in the area they're in doesn't grow brighter than dim light.

**Unbodied Possession**   An invidiak leaves no body behind when using [_possession_](/Spells.aspx?ID=225).

</column>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-28" />
name: Invidiak
